title: Claudia
author: Michael Hall
description:
Claudia, also known as Claudia and David, is an American old-time radio drama. One version was broadcast on CBS July 4, 1941 - September 26, 1941[1] and another was syndicated in 1947. Claudia and David were first heard on radio in a segment on the June 6, 1941, episode of The Kate Smith Hour. At that time, Claudia had just fallen in love with David. When Smith's program went off for the summer, the 10-minute segments were expanded to fill her time slot. The couple's wedding occurred on the July 11, 1941, episode. The characters first appeared in ten short stories by Rose Franken that were published in Redbook magazine from October 1938 through August 1939. Claudia, the Story of a Marriage, a book that included those stories and additional material, was published in 1939. It was followed by other books about the couple. The program focused on the lives of David and Claudia Naughton, a young married couple. At 18, Claudia's close ties to her mother threatened the future of her marriage to David. The couple also had to deal with financial problems, getting used to each other, and other adjustments typical of a new marriage. In the first episode, David's life was threatened by a sudden illness, resulting in increased tension between David's mother and the rest of the family, while it strengthened the bonds among David, Claudia, and her mother. General Foods was the sponsor, promoting Grape-Nuts and Grape-Nuts Flakes.

title: Calling All Cars
author: shanto
description:
Calling all Cars ran from 1933-1939. It's considered to be the inspiration for the old-time radio great, Dragnet. Calling All Cars chronicles the L.A. Police Department cases. Although this is an earlier version of the same type of show Dragnet was, there are some differences. Calling All Cars was introduced at a time when Americans became much more mobile because of the automobile. Criminals could get into a waiting car, and if they got lucky enough, get lost in traffic! Dragnet came out much later, but they both gave a thorough and detailed account of the crime and the criminals behind the crime. Although during the time that Calling All Cars aired, criminals were sometimes glamorized, like Bonnie and Clyde and John Dillinger. Whereas during the later police and crime shows that aired on old time radio, that wasn't so much the case anymore. With Dragnet in mind, there is another major difference, Dragnet used the same Police Officers each episode. Calling All Cars used different Police Officers each episode. There were only two recurring characters in Calling All Cars, the narrator Charles Lindsey, and real-life LAPD dispatcher Jessie Rosenquist.

title: Morning Rounds: Opening Up the World of Bhakti Through Japa Meditation
author: Hari Prasada Das (Upbuild)
description:
Like a doctor making rounds to support healing, Morning Rounds is about tending to the soul. Through the chanting of the Holy Names, we gradually quiet the mind, soften the pull of the ego, and reconnect with our deeper spiritual nature.

Drawn from our weekly japa meditation gatherings at Upbuild, these reflections offer a few minutes of inspiration to support your meditation and begin your day with greater clarity, humility, and joy.

In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna describes japa, the chanting of the Holy Names, as the highest form of sacrifice. This timeless practice has the power to transform our consciousness through the simple act of returning, again and again, to the sound of the Holy Names.

Whether you are new to chanting or have an established practice, these reflections are an invitation to deepen your relationship with the maha-mantra:

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna
Krishna Krishna Hare Hare
Hare Rama Hare Rama
Rama Rama Hare Hare

Through consistent practice and the support of community, we begin to experience a deepening sense of joy and connection that arises simply from hearing and chanting these sacred names.

title: Be Gentle With Me
author: Christie Quarton
description:
Hey there, beautiful souls! Welcome to "Be Gentle With Me," your cozy corner of the podcast world where it's totally okay to not have this whole life thing figured out. Feeling like you need a big ol' hug for your heart while you navigate life's rollercoaster?

You're in the right place! Every Sunday, I'm here to wrap you up in audio comfort as we chat about healing, growing, and being a little kinder to ourselves - especially when life feels like a pop quiz you didn't study for.

What's on the menu? Glad you asked!

• We'll laugh about those awkward "oops" moments in our healing journeys

• Share some "why didn't I think of that?" tips for dealing with everyday stress and past hurts

• Hear from real folks who've been there, done that, and got the emotional t-shirt

• And yep, we're totally gonna explore how to radiate more confidence (spoiler: it involves lots of self-compassion)

Whether you're dealing with some heavy stuff from the past, trying to boost your self-worth, or just figuring out how to adult without a manual, remember: you're not alone in this wild ride. We're all healing and growing together, one wobbly step at a time.

So grab your favorite comfy blanket, curl up with your beverage of choice, and join our little family of gentle souls. New episodes drop every Sunday – because, let's face it, we could all use a little extra love to start the week!

#BeGentleWithMe #HealingJourney #SelfCompassionSunday

title: Santa Barbara Medical Spa & Aesthetics Guide:  Cosmetic Treatments, Laser Skin Care & Body Contouring
author: Riviera Medical Spa
description:
Santa Barbara Medical Spa & Aesthetics Guide is a physician-developed podcast for anyone in the Santa Barbara, Montecito, Carpinteria, and Santa Ynez Valley area exploring medical spa treatments, cosmetic procedures, and non-surgical aesthetics.

If you've been wondering whether laser skin resurfacing, CoolSculpting, Botox, dermal fillers, RF microneedling, or a facelift might be right for you.  This is where you get real answers based on physician expertise, not a sales pitch.

Each episode breaks down a specific treatment or aesthetic concern in plain language: what it does, how it works, who it's best for, what results to expect, and what questions to ask before booking. Topics include:

  • Laser treatments — CoolPeel CO2 resurfacing, IPL photofacial, and Avava laser for all skin tones
  • Body contouring — CoolSculpting and CoolSculpting Elite for non-surgical fat reduction
  • Skin tightening — Ultherapy focused ultrasound, Vivace RF microneedling, and Renuvion J-Plasma
  • Injectables — Botox, Daxxify, Juvederm, Sculptra, and dermal filler treatments
  • Surgical options — DeepFrame Facelift, deep plane facelift, eyelid surgery, rhinoplasty, and body procedures
  • Skin health — acne scarring, pigmentation, sun damage, texture, and anti-aging skincare

Every episode is developed from the clinical expertise and patient education content of Dr. Adam Lowenstein, a board-certified plastic surgeon and founder of Riviera Medical Spa at Montecito Plastic Surgery in Santa Barbara, California. With decades of experience in both surgical and non-surgical aesthetics, Dr. Lowenstein's knowledge is the foundation for everything you hear on this show; the same expertise behind one of the Central Coast's leading aesthetic practices.

Episode production uses AI technology, developed from physician-reviewed clinical content, and are designed to give you the kind of clear, trustworthy information that helps you make confident decisions about your care, whether you're exploring your very first med spa treatment or researching your next procedure.

New to medical spa treatments? Start at Episode 1. Already researching a specific procedure? Search the episode library by treatment name.

Riviera Medical Spa at Montecito Plastic Surgery

author: Heather Gilmour - Mom Coach
description:
Hey mama, are you tired of feeling constant stress and overwhelm? Are you drowning under the never-ending list of household chores and feeling pulled in every direction to meet everyone’s needs? Do you feel like a chicken running around with it’s head cut off, trying to normalize the chaos without tapping out?
Well, I’m so glad you are here! This podcast will help you have more peace and joy in your home and spirit, give practical household management tips, strengthen relationships and encourage you mama’s heart!
Hi, I’m Heather. A mom, wife, Gigi and Jesus follower. I have 6 kids and 7 grandkids and have homeschooled and run a big household for decades. My biggest win is that all of my kids still like me and love Jesus!
I struggled for years with stress, overwhelm, parenting challenges, relationship strain, fear and worry ALL while trying to keep my household running.
I found that by asking for help and encouragement from older moms and trying and failing ALOT, the Lord equipped me to have more confidence, effective routings and strategies that gave me better relationships and more peace in my home and heart.
I am here to encourage you, to share things that worked for me, to lighten your days amidst all the hard, and to help YOU manage your big family better!
 So if you are ready to be encouraged, say good-bye to overwhelm, get practical tips for running your big household, create solid connections with ALL your people, find rest for your weary soul, and hopefully laugh a little- this podcast is for you!!
So pop some headphones in while you sweep, meal prep, and fold laundry and let’s do this!

title: Brazoria County Chronicles: Pioneers, Plantations, and the Birth of Texas
author: Varner-Hogg Plantation and Levi Jordan Plantation State Historic Sites
description:
Monroe Edwards was one of the most notorious figures in early Texas history. He was an illegal slave trader, con artist, and the central figure in a sensational 1842 trial that captured national attention. Edwards smuggled hundreds of captive Africans into Brazoria County during the 1830s and sold them off to other plantation owners in the area. Edwards crimes would eventually lead to his downfall.
Tune into our podcast, Brazoria County Chronicles: The Scandalous Rise and Fall of Monroe Edwards to learn more about Edwards and his impact on Brazoria County. 
Episodes air monthly summer 2026.

title: Bad Blood: The Diary of a Cancer
author: Jamie East
description:
At some point 1 in 2 people will be affected by cancer.


I was the same as you whenever I read that stat, I just always felt sorry for the other guy. Plot twist… you will occasionally be the other guy.


In June 2025, after a completely random visit to the doctor, I was diagnosed with Chronic Myeloid Leukaemia, which despite its fun sounding name, isn’t exactly a barrel of laughs. Sh** got real quite quickly.


Up until then, I was a healthy, active, happy and successful man, heading into middle-age with carefree abandon. Life has a funny way of humbling you.


As someone who’s lived their life in front of a camera, microphone or audience, my knee-jerk reaction was to monetise the situation  switch on the recorder and talk myself through it - making sense of my diagnosis (what even is Leukaemia?) the impending chemotherapy, my family and how our lives had been flipped on it’s head.


Look, there are tons of these podcasts out there, by some incredible people with stories far more horrific than mine (spoiler alert - I’m typing myself this a year later) - but if a perplexed, foul-mouthed audio diary from someone who once interviewed Jedward in a carpark is your thing, you’re in the right place.


Jamie

author: Matthew Richardson
description:
With Angela Barnes & Matt Richardson


Stand-up Comedians and Mates Angela Barnes and Matt Richardson have both found a love for gardening, but have absolutely no clue what they're doing. If you love getting your knees dirty and your fingers green but are gardening totally blind - this is the show for you. A judgement free show, where no question is too stupid and no failure is too embarrassing. Angela & Matt are trying to make their new hobby a little less snobby. Each week they're joined by guests from the gardening and comedy world to chat all things green.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Money Moves with Faride
author: Faride Sisniega
description:
Welcome to Money Moves with Faride — the podcast where smart women (and bold men!) come to take control of their financial future.

I’m Faride Sisniega, founder of Expert Agents Tax & Business Solutions and a passionate financial educator partnering with the How Money Works movement. In every episode, I break down what you really need to know about money, taxes, business, and wealth-building strategies—without the fluff, the jargon, or the confusion.

Whether you’re starting over, starting a business, or just starting to take your finances seriously, this is your go-to place to learn how money really works—and how to make it work for you.

Tune in, take notes, and get ready to turn knowledge into power.
 Let’s build a life of financial clarity, freedom, and confidence—together.

title: The Dog’s Voice
author: Dog Breeding Reform Group
description:
The Dog’s Voice is a podcast about dog health, puppy care, and responsible breeding. Hosted by Ben the Vet and brought to you by the Dog Breeding Reform Group, each episode explores the science, welfare, and real-life decisions that shape healthier dogs.

Through expert conversations, we cover topics including genetics, breeding practices, common health conditions, and how to choose and care for a dog responsibly. Whether you’re looking for a puppy, already living with a dog, or working with dogs professionally, this podcast offers clear, practical guidance to help you make informed decisions.

The Dog’s Voice is here to support better breeding, better welfare, and a healthier future for dogs and the people who care for them.

title: NY Weekly Record
author: Michele Evans
description: NY Weekly Record brings you sharp, independent New York City reporting with real stories, local impact, and NYC always at the center. Hosted by Michele Evans, the show covers courts, crime, justice, public safety, city politics, Rikers Island, culture, and the moments shaping life across New York. It’s part newsroom, part public-interest watchdog, part lived-experience commentary - built for people who want the story behind the headline and the humanity behind the system.

title: GA3-240202501-AA1-EV02. Audio
author: Sandra Milena Zapata Gonzalez
description: I believe that, both academically and professionally, we must maintain a positive and proactive attitude toward the challenges that may arise. We have to be responsible for our tasks and commitments, and we  should manage our time effectively to meet those responsibilities. We  should  develop strong communication and teamwork skills while always respecting others' different viewpoints and fostering a healthy environment based on tolerance and respect.   We must always act with honesty, ethics, and integrity, and we have to follow the established rules and regulations. In addition, we should remain willing to learn and improve continuously. We should also share the knowledge and experience we acquire whenever it is necessary to help others and contribute to a positive academic and professional environment.

title: Peace Love Joy... Find Yours
author: Melissa Ocean
description:
Welcome home! It’s lovely to have you here. A space that holds the voices that remind you of you. 
Your inner peace, your love of yourself and the simple inner joy that brings to you. A love letter from us to you reminding you of your magnificence and power in reconnecting with yourself.
Our sole purpose is to bring you home to you; connecting with the one that matters most, and healing along the journey of life’s waves of transitions. Follow us on our journey to remind you how brightly you shine.

title: Don't Pay Retail: Mortgage & Home Loan Tips with Ty Keller
author: TyClosesLoans.com
description: Most people overpay for their mortgage and never know it. Don't Pay Retail is where wholesale mortgage broker Ty Keller pulls back the curtain on home loans, in plain English, the stuff banks won't tell you. First-time buying, refinancing, self-employed and bank-statement loans, jumbo, investor and doctor loans, and how to actually get approved across Cumming, Forsyth County, and North Atlanta. title: Frass Happens
author: Frass Happens Podcast
description:
Join Casey and Katie, a couple of entomologists, educators, and best friends, as they explore the planet’s largest group of animals – invertebrates! In each biweekly episode of Frass Happens, the two share wild facts and stories about the “little things that run the world” and hope to turn their listeners into lovers of ALL animals (not just the furry ones).🐝

From incredible true stories and scientific discoveries to conservation efforts and ocean adventures, each episode explores the lives of some of the ocean’s most intelligent and beloved animals. Whether you’re a lifelong marine-life enthusiast or simply curious about the sea, there’s always a new tale waiting beneath the waves.

title: Africa Revealed with Dr. Matt Jensen

description: Africa Revealed takes you beyond the postcard — into the wildlife, people, cultures, and real stories of East Africa. Hosted by Dr. Matt Jensen, safari guide and humanitarian, bringing you insider knowledge from Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania & beyond.

title: 10 Keys to Thrive
author: Jim Krigbaum
description:
10 Keys to Thrive is a business and leadership podcast for entrepreneurs, executives, and growth-minded professionals who want real-world strategies—not theory—to build successful, scalable ventures.

Hosted by global business strategist Jim Krigbaum, this podcast draws on experience from working in 80+ countries and advising thousands of entrepreneurs across international markets. Each episode delivers practical insights on entrepreneurship, business strategy, leadership, market expansion, and execution—so you can stop guessing and start growing.


At the core of the show is Jim’s proven CHARM DANCE framework:

  • Communication
  • Honesty
  • Appropriate & Consistent Quality
  • Risk
  • Markets
  • Details
  • Advantages
  • Networks
  • Customer
  • Execution


You’ll learn how to:

  • Identify the right product-market fit
  • Build a competitive advantage that lasts
  • Navigate global markets and scaling challenges
  • Make better decisions around risk and execution
  • Align what you produce with what customers actually want to buy


Whether you're launching a startup, scaling a company, or leading a team, 10 Keys to Thrive gives you the clarity, tools, and frameworks to grow with confidence.

If you're serious about business growth, leadership development, and entrepreneurial success, this podcast is your roadmap.


👉 Subscribe now and start building a business—and life—that thrives.

title: Relate: Living Room Chats
author: Gigi VanDame
description: Have you ever felt like you simply don't know how to grow closer to God? Same. For years I lived out a surface-level faith, I had the church attendance and the prayers that felt like they hit the ceiling. But something was missing. That's exactly why Relate exists. In this podcast, we're going deep into who God is, how He relates to us, and how we can move from a distant faith into one that feels alive. Each episode is short, honest, and built to give you something real to walk away with. It's time to Relate.

title: Unmetered Podcast
author: Milbank Manufacturing
description:
Unmetered is Milbank’s podcast dedicated to the people, ideas, and innovations shaping the electrical industry. From evolving technologies and industry trends to the real world challenges faced by utilities, contractors, and manufacturers, Unmetered delivers insightful conversations grounded in real world experience.

Hosted by industry voices and featuring guests from across the field, each episode takes a practical, straightforward look at how power gets delivered and where the industry is headed next. Whether you’re on the job, in the office, or on the road, Unmetered keeps you connected to what matters most in the world of electrical infrastructure

title: The "Becoming HER" Podcast
author: Shauna Nolan
description:
Becoming HER is the audio version of the newsletter — for the woman who’s done surviving quietly and ready to come home to herself.
If my writing hits you in the chest but you don’t always have time to sit and read, this is for you. Pop in your earbuds on the way to work, while you’re cleaning, driving, walking — and let me read it to you with the tone, emotion, and truth it was written in.
Each episode is part permission slip, part reset, part “okay… now what?” 
You’ll get mindset shifts, identity upgrades, and practical reminders to stop abandoning yourself and start becoming the woman you
Want the written version? 
Every episode of “Becoming HER” pairs with a weekly newsletter and written for the woman doing the work in real time. 
Subscribe for free at 
https://open.substack.com/pub/shaunanolan

title: Empower Her Vibes
author: kolanda
description:
This podcast is for the woman who is tired.


Not sleepy tired. Soul tired. The kind of tired that does not go away with sleep -- that lives in your chest and shows up in the way you answer when someone asks how you are doing. "I'm fine." When you are so far from fine that fine sounds like a language you forgot how to speak.


EmpowerHer Vibes is for the woman who has been giving everyone her best and keeping nothing for herself. The woman who says yes when she means no. The woman who has been showing up for everyone else while quietly disappearing from her own life.


Hosted by Kolanda -- AI R&B artist, creator of the Finding Me album, and a woman who spent years shrinking herself to keep everyone else comfortable. She stopped. And every Wednesday she sits down with you to talk about what that actually looks like.

What you will hear every Wednesday:
 -- The real truth about why women shrink -- and what it costs them
 -- The psychology behind self-worth, self-abandonment, and real self-love
 -- Tools you can use this week -- not someday, not when you feel ready -- this week
 -- A featured song from the Finding Me album that carries the emotional truth of the episode


 -- A community of women doing this work together

EmpowerHer Vibes is not a podcast about having it all figured out. It is a podcast about the ongoing, imperfect, and deeply courageous work of choosing yourself. Again and again. Until it becomes who you are.

New episodes every Wednesday.


 Free resources at beacons.ai/empowervibes


Know Your Worth. Stop Shrinking. Stop Abandoning Yourself.

title: Delivery Point Podcast
author: Don Morley
description: Delivery Point Podcast is where logistics finally gets its moment in the spotlight — whether it wants it or not. We dive into the stories, mishaps, small miracles and “you won’t believe this” moments that keep the industry moving. From drivers with legendary tales to founders who built empires out of chaos, we bring you honest conversations without the corporate fluff. It’s smart, funny, occasionally sarcastic, and always real. If you’ve ever worked in logistics — or survived it — this is your new favourite listen.

title: 4Students
author: 4Students
description:
Welcome to 4Students, a podcast created by TDSB students for TDSB students. Each episode features a new group of students discussing wellness topics that matter most to them. Our host, school and clinical psychologist Dr. Aarti Kumar, helps amplify the voices of Toronto-based students and offers thoughtful insight along the way.
At 4Students, we believe that no matter our age, we are lifelong learners—and that there is so much wisdom to be gained from young people.

title: Math Beyond School
author: NYS Network for Youth Success
description:
Math Beyond School is a podcast miniseries sponsored by the New York State Network for Youth Success. This show is designed to support afterschool and out-of-school time programs. 
Over three episodes, the show's host, Sara Kobilka, will discuss 1) Math's place in STEM (science, technology, engineering and math), 2) The challenge of math identity for students AND educators, 3) Moving beyond the stereotypical "math is everywhere" message to find creative ways to show students that math and mathematical thinking are relevant in our daily lives.

description: Welcome to the intersection of current events and ancient truth. We’re two pastors who vent about the state of the world while teaching the Word of God. Our conversations are unscripted, unpredictable, and always honest. You never know what's coming next—and neither do we.

title: Winging it With Wendy
author: Wendy Burton
description:
Real people. Real stories. Real Angels.
Join award-winning author Wendy S. Burton (Amy and the Angels) as she shares stories of guidance, comfort, and curiosity connected to Angels—from her own life and from listeners. Each episode creates space to wonder if we’re really winging it alone… or if something is walking right alongside us.
For the curious and open-minded.
https://www.wendysburton.com/

title: The Market Mosaic
author: Accuvest Investment Team
description:
The Market Mosaic is the flagship podcast from Accuvest Global Advisors, where we connect the pieces shaping the global investment landscape. Rather than focusing on headlines or short-term market noise, we assemble the market mosaic—bringing together macroeconomic trends, AI innovation, corporate earnings, capital flows, sector leadership, investor sentiment, and emerging technologies—to reveal the bigger picture.

Each episode delivers thoughtful analysis of the world's most compelling investment opportunities, timeless businesses, disruptive innovations, and secular themes that can shape portfolios for years to come. Whether we're exploring the future of artificial intelligence, the companies building tomorrow's infrastructure, or the world's greatest compounders, our goal is simple: provide investors with the perspective needed to make better long-term decisions.

If you're an investor who believes the best opportunities come from understanding how the pieces fit together—not just reacting to the latest headline—The Market Mosaic is your guide to seeing what others miss. Join Accuvest Global Advisors as we uncover the trends driving tomorrow's markets and help you connect the dots before the rest of the market does. Important: This is not financial advice. These discussions are for educational and informational purposes only.

title: Gas Connections Adelaide Guide
author: Josh Monsigneur
description: A practical, easy-to-follow podcast about gas connections Adelaide, covering the basics of gas installation, safety, planning, and what property owners should know before starting a project. Each episode is designed to explain the process in simple language, making it useful for homeowners, renovators, landlords, and business owners who want clearer answers without the jargon.

title: Biblical Woman and Wifehood Podcast
author: Amber-Christiana P
description:
This is a space for women in every season of life to grow deeper in Christ and become everything God has created them to be.
Whether you're a teenage girl 🌸, single 🤍, dating 💍, in courtship, engaged 💍, newly married, or have been married for years, you're welcome girl!!
Together we'll have honest, girl chat conversations about…
🤍 Womanhood & biblical femininity 
💍 Wifehood & preparing for marriage 
🌿 Singleness, dating, courtship & intentional relationships
🫶 Godly friendships & real sisterhood
📖 Bible studies & devotionals 
🙏 Prayer & intimacy with God 
🔥 Spiritual growth & discipleship 
🩷 Healing, emotional health & becoming whole in Christ 
🧠 Mental health through a biblical lens 
🌸 Hormones, PMS & caring for the body God gave us 
🏡 Homemaking, cooking & everyday life 
✨ Inward adornment, beauty & modesty 
🌎 Purpose, ministry & living for God's glory 
☕ And of course... lots of cozy girl-to-girl chats along the way.
We'll celebrate victories, walk through growing pains, talk about the lessons God has taught me (including the mistakes 😅), and learn how to live faithfully in every season.
My heart isn't to build perfect Christian women. It's to point women back to Jesus. 🤍
Every episode is rooted in and Abiding in Him, and scripture. This podcast is reated to encourage you to know God more deeply, walk confidently in life, and live out your faith in everyday life.
If you've been looking for a place that feels like sitting down with the big sister or friend in Christ who is called to walk right alongside you.
So grab your Bible 📖, your favorite coffee or tea ☕, take that grocery store drive... on your way to serve your family?? churchhh?? ... let's go together 
Welcome, I'm so grateful you're here. 🤍✨

title: VAR From Perfect
author: VAR From Perfect
description:
Football is never perfect, and neither are we.
Join us each week as we break down the biggest stories from around the football world: from Premier League drama and transfer madness, to Champions League nights, international tournaments, controversial VAR decisions, and everything in between.
Expect passionate debates, questionable takes, football banter, and the occasional prediction that ages terribly.
Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@varfromperfect
Instagram: @VarFromPerfect

title: Truly Transformative
author: Caryn Lee and Adriana McLane
description:
Aligning culture and strategy for lasting results.
Most strategic plans fail — not because the strategy is wrong, but because the culture can't support it. The Truly Transformative Podcast tackles this head-on.
Hosts Adriana McLane and Caryn Lee bring decades of hands-on experience helping organizations build bold strategies and the cultures that make them stick. Each episode delivers practical frameworks, real-world case studies, and honest conversations about what it actually takes to transform an organization from the inside out.
This isn't theory. It's what works.

title: Becoming Her
author: Yacer Abdelaziz
description:
Becoming Her is a podcast for women who are done playing small. Each episode is an honest conversation about confidence, psychology, and the quiet inner work behind real transformation — the kind that doesn't come from affirmations, but from action. If you're ready to stop waiting to feel ready and start becoming the woman you were always meant to be, you're in the right place.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Sesh With Sav
author: savvas
description: Just a man on the search for a good story and even better banter. All the best conversations happen on the sesh.

title: Conservative Shillcast
author: Senator-Elect
description:
Welcome to the show where the gloves come off, the spin keeps spinning, and conservative talking points are repackaged as fearless independence. After twenty years in and around Western conservative politics, the host is finally ready to challenge the establishment - provided the establishment in question is Liberal, NDP, Indigenous, eastern, or insufficiently deferential to Alberta.

If you are looking for a conservative echo chamber, you are in exactly the right place. Politicians will be held to one relentless standard: whether they agree with the host. This is not about making friends in high places. It is about inviting them onto the podcast, praising their courage, and helping explain why whatever they just did was actually Ottawa’s fault.


From the desks of punditry to the halls of Parliament, people pay attention because confidence is often mistaken for insight. In partnership with a rapidly growing conservative content mill, the show breaks down the data, busts the bullshit, and occasionally produces some of its own.

Tune in every week for the context you can get almost anywhere else, the grievances you have heard a thousand times before, and the raw truth about how the West is really run - by people who insist they have no power while appearing on podcasts to explain how much influence they have.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: TheLostBroadcast
author: TheLostBroadcast
description:
Welcome to The Lost Broadcast.
This podcast is home to my longer horror stories and narrations. While shorter stories, and other content may be posted elsewhere, this is where you'll find the full-length experiences designed for late-night listening, long drives, and sleepless nights.
Featuring original horror stories, creepy pastas, Reddit stories, viewer submissions, disturbing true stories, and unsettling tales from around the internet.
If you'd like to submit a story for narration, you can send it to: lostfilesinbox@gmail.com
full videos: https://www.youtube.com/@Thelostbroadcasttv

title: The Tomcat Catchup
author: Robert Doyle
description:
A weekly news radio show that covers national and international politics, along with things happening around the campus of Thiel College! Schedule for the radio show TBD for the Fall!
Hosted by WXTC Radio News Director Robert Doyle.

title: Live Read: Daily Fantasy Football
author: DS Studios
description: Live Read is a daily podcast covering everything in fantasy football. Hosts Mac and Jess bring you the latest and greatest news the moment it breaks, plus top ranked players, trends to watch, risks, trades, adds and drops, coaching changes, and everything in between. If it moves the fantasy world, you will hear it here first. Our goal is simple: be the fastest place to get new fantasy football news as it happens, every single day. No fluff, just the insight you need to stay ahead and win your league. Follow Live Read and never miss a beat.

title: Organizing to Win: The Art & Science of Running for Office
author: Jimmy Tickey
description:
The American story has been written by the people who show up, speak out, and organize, especially in challenging times. Organizing to Win is the audio version of the top-selling book teaching you how to enter the political arena, manage a campaign, and run for public office.


Written - and narrated - by Jimmy Tickey, a trusted political voice in Connecticut, providing guidance to local, state, and federal campaigns. Blending political strategy with public service, Jimmy Tickey is the Vice Chair of the Connecticut Democratic Party and member of the Democratic National Committee where he leads candidate trainings. A campaign manager, elected official, party leader, and staff member to government officials, Jimmy understands firsthand where politics meets policy: organizing.

title: Beyond the Boardroom with Louisa Joseph
author: Louisa Joseph
description: Have you wondered what real human leadership is about? ..

title: Everyone Deserves a Lighthouse
author: Chris Fitzwater
description: Everyone Deserves a Lighthouse, hosted by Chris Fitzwater, exists to help people take the next faithful step toward Jesus. Each episode offers clear Biblical truth, practical Apostolic teaching, and honest conversation about faith, discipleship, church life, leadership, and living for God in the real world. A podcast from Lighthouse Charlottesville.

title: Center & Circle
author: The Tengu Center for Community Wellness
description: Center and Circle is a monthly podcast by the Tengu Center for Community Wellness that explores the connection between self and community. Through reflective conversations, we examine how personal experiences and collective wellness shape one another. It’s a space where inner awareness meets outward impact—where the center meets the circle.

title: Humanities Podcast Project
author: Grady Riester
description: School Project

title: It Would Be a Crime
author: Sid & Erinn
description:
A former homicide and narcotics detective tells his daughter the real stories behind 23 years in law enforcement — for the first time. True crime, undercover operations, murder investigations, faith, and the emotional cost behind the badge. 


Instagram: @authorsidcovington 

 TikTok: @authorsidcovington 

 YouTube: It Would Be A Crime Podcast

title: Face Facts
author: Gemma Copper + Faye Hayes
description:
Welcome to Face Facts.


A podcast where aesthetics meets real life.


Hosted by two friends, mums and aesthetics practitioners, Face Facts is a refreshingly honest conversation about faces, ageing, confidence, relationships, work, motherhood, wellness and everything else life throws our way.


Some weeks we’ll be talking filler, Botox, industry myths and the latest trends. Other weeks we’ll be talking about friendships, hormones, business, parenting, products we’re loving, things we’re questioning and the realities of trying to juggle it all.


We’ll be separating the facts from the bullshit, sharing stories from clinic and beyond, debating hot takes and saying the things most people only admit behind closed doors.


No scripts. No lectures. No pretending we’ve got it all figured out.


Just honest conversations, strong opinions, plenty of laughs and the occasional uncomfortable truth.


Because life isn’t just about how you look.


But let’s be honest… sometimes it helps.




Produced by Holly Rushton.

title: APD Presents The White Whale: The Chelsea Yasser Story
author: Aurora Police Department
description: Ten years. Ten years since 21-year-old Arizona native Chelsea Yasser was left for dead in a parking lot -- her last moments captured on security footage. Her killer, sitting in the driver's seat of a maroon van, has never been identified. Now, in a first for the Aurora (CO) Police Department, a podcast has been created to bring listeners inside the investigation, to help find Chelsea's killer, and to close a case that has haunted so many investigators over the years it has become known as The White Whale, a reference to the infamous foe of Captain Ahab in the classic novel 'Moby Dick."

title: The Soft Parts Podcast
author: Nik and Nhi
description:
Hosted by Haitian-American and Vietnamese-American best friends Nik and Nhi, The Soft Parts Podcast explores identity, relationships, healing, culture, intimacy, burnout, vulnerability, and the beautifully imperfect process of becoming, one honest conversation at a time. 
Our hope is to create a space that feels emotionally immersive, warm, reflective, and deeply human, inviting thoughtful conversations that stay with you long after they end.
We hope you'll join us as we navigate the beautiful mess of being human.
So settle in, stay curious, and let's explore the soft parts together.

title: The Presence & Pursuit Podcast
author: Lauren Pugh
description:
What if your honest pursuit of Jesus has somehow turned into a relentless, exhausting list of expectations? That a lot of what you’ve been told about following Jesus isn't what you've experienced modeled around you.


The Presence and Pursuit Podcast is a faith-based show for anyone hungry for more of Jesus - not more of religion. The one who knows in their bones that something is missing -  not in an arrogant way, but from a place of holy desperation. 


Hosted by Lauren, a photographer, storyteller, and Bible teacher, this podcast is for the burned out, the curious, and the sincere seekers who are done with performance Christianity and ready to experience a vibrant walk with God in Spirit and Truth.


Each episode blends scripture-grounded teaching with the surprising ways neuroscience, nature, and everyday life confirms that walking with God looks like pursuing Him from a place of secured identity and honesty.


Here, we slow down. We strip back the noise. We return to simple rhythms and practices that have always drawn people closer to God - as we rediscover the freedom we experience when we pursue Jesus > everything else.


Topics include:

• Presence > Performance

• Getting back to the basics of belief

• How science and scripture can help us heal

• Hearing from God, trusting your discernment, & applying wisdom

• Identity in Christ -  having a kingdom mindset

• Uprooting faulty theology and the lies that may be holding us back

• Our inner desire for wholeness and peace


If you’re rebuilding your faith, deepening your walk, or simply tired of the noise - this is your space to breathe, seek, and pursue Jesus over everything else.


New episodes dropping June 2026. Subscribe now and start your pursuit.

title: Wilmington Life
author: Gen Parker
description:
Welcome to Wilmington Life: Your Gateway to the Heart of Wilmington, NCAre you ready to dive deep into the vibrant life of Wilmington, North Carolina? Whether you're a long-time resident, a newcomer, or just curious about this beautiful coastal city, our podcast, Wilmington Life, is your ultimate guide. We bring you closer to the stories, experiences, and insights that make Wilmington not just a place to live, but a place to love.

Why Wilmington Life?Wilmington Life is more than just a podcast; it's a celebration of the city's rich history, diverse culture, and the unique lifestyle it offers. Each episode is carefully crafted to explore different aspects of life in Wilmington, from the hidden gems of local dining to the serene beauty of our beaches, and everything in between. Our goal is to provide you with a comprehensive understanding of what makes Wilmington such a special place to call home.What You’ll Discover
  • Local Lifestyle: Get a taste of what it's like to live in Wilmington, with episodes that highlight local events, recreational activities, and the best spots for shopping, dining, and entertainment. Whether you're looking for the perfect weekend getaway or the next must-visit restaurant, we've got you covered.
  • City Insights: We dive into the history and culture of Wilmington, sharing fascinating stories and lesser-known facts that even locals might not know. From the city's historic landmarks to the thriving arts scene, Wilmington Life is your go-to source for all things Wilmington.
  • Community Voices: Hear from local residents, business owners, and community leaders as they share their personal experiences and insights. Their stories not only reflect the spirit of Wilmington but also provide a unique perspective on what it's like to live and work in this charming city.
  • Living Well: Discover tips and advice on living your best life in Wilmington. From health and wellness to home and garden, we cover topics that matter to you and your family. Our episodes are designed to help you make the most of what Wilmington has to offer.
Why You Should Tune InAt Wilmington Life, we believe that everyone has a story, and Wilmington is full of them. By tuning in, you'll become part of a community that celebrates the city's uniqueness and the people who make it thrive. Whether you're looking to explore new places, connect with your neighbors, or simply enjoy some local storytelling, our podcast is here to inspire and entertain.

Subscribe and Stay ConnectedDon’t miss out on the latest episodes of Wilmington Life! By subscribing, you’ll receive notifications about new episodes, special guest appearances, and exclusive content that you won’t find anywhere else. Whether you're commuting, working out, or relaxing at home, Wilmington Life is the perfect companion to keep you connected to the city you love.Join Us on This JourneyWe invite you to embark on this journey with us and explore Wilmington in a way you've never experienced before. Wilmington Life is more than just a podcast; it's a celebration of our city and the amazing people who call it home. So, what are you waiting for? Hit that subscribe button, and let’s start exploring together!

title: Disability Fail 101
author: 2RPH
description: Join host Mel Harrison and various guests as they share stories of the well-meaning but totally missing the mark moments and mishaps surrounding disability.

title: figuring out my 20s
author: Brooklyn Price
description: Figuring Out My 20s is a solo podcast about navigating life in your 20s—relationships, work, growth, and everything in between. I don’t have it all figured out, but I’m learning as I go and sharing it in real time. If you’re trying to find your way, chase your goals, and trust your own timing, this is for you.

title: Figuring It Out in Real Time
author: Kat Monaco
description:
Most people think they’ll recognize the moment their life changes.
They won’t.
Figuring It Out in Real Time is a deeply personal story about friendship, marriage, betrayal, resilience, and the complicated reality of rebuilding your life when the people you trusted most become the source of your greatest pain.
Through humor, honesty, and reflection, Kat Monaco takes listeners inside a journey through community, loss, forgiveness, heartbreak, and self-discovery.
Because life doesn’t come with a roadmap.
Most of us are just figuring it out in real time.

From the quiet tech wars over semiconductor dominance to classified Cold War espionage and engineering marvels that changed

description: From the Florida Everglades to the fine print of the U.S. Constitution, this episode challenges us to look closer at the fabric of American identity. Tune in to discover the history you might have missed in school, and the tools you need to navigate the world today.

title: Life After the Death: She Had to Go
author: Tamara Keara
description:
The version of you that got you here cannot take you where you are destined to go. Period.

Welcome to Life After the Death: She Had to Go, the official podcast space for Bē Ntentional. Hosted by Tamara Keara, this show is a raw, uncompromised masterclass in personal evolution, boundary execution, and shifting into a luxury, intentional lifestyle.

Every single episode is an audit of what happens when you finally stop playing small, cut ties with out of bounds energy, and execute the ultimate standard of living and working with absolute intention. This isn't recycled advice, we are diving straight into original, proprietary frameworks designed to help you blueprint your transformation and master your personal authority. The old version of you has to be completely eliminated for the vision to exist.

If you are ready to stop drifting, master your personal authority, and design a life completely by blueprint instead of by accident, you are exactly where you belong. Turn the volume up, lock the door, and pull up your chair.

It’s time to live with intention.

author: The Nosebleeds

title: MavenHeart
author: Nicole Ternay
description:
Welcome to the MavenHeart Podcast!
The MavenHeart Podcast is for the woman who has lived through the breaking point… when everything familiar falls apart and your identity goes with it.
You’ll hear real stories from guests who faced that moment head-on… who felt the loss, the confusion, the unraveling… and refused to rebuild a life that no longer fit. And in every conversation, I’ll bring you back to you… cutting through the noise, challenging the patterns, and showing you what it actually takes to rebuild from your core.
This is about what happens after. When you rise, redefine yourself, and step into a version of you that your old life could never hold.
Podcast drops every Tuesday! Hit subscribe and join us each week!
Learn more at nicoleternay.com

title: The Robot Investor
author: The Robot Investor
description: The Robot Investor breaks down the fast-moving world of robotics and humanoid-robot investing for everyday investors — no finance degree required. Each week, analyst Elena and curious investor Theo unpack the headlines in a Friday news roundup and take a closer look at one company in twice-weekly deep dives, weighing the bull case against the bear case in plain English. From Tesla's Optimus and Nvidia to the supply-chain names quietly powering the boom, we cut through the hype to ask what would actually have to go right — and what could go wrong. Honest, balanced, and a little bit fun. Both ho

author: National Council of Farmer Cooperatives
description: Running a farmer co-op today means navigating a complicated set of pressures from global markets and evolving public policy to building a skilled workforce and adopting new technology – all while continuing to meet the expectations of the farmer-owners you serve. Every month, Cooperative Voice brings you the cooperative leaders, policymakers, and experts who have something important to say about the business and policy environment shaping America's farmer-owned co-ops.

title: Pear Naked Truth
author: adampears
description:
Autistic. Gay. Black. Judge Judy plaintiff. Former Googler. 

From his sunroom in NYC, Adam Pears shares hard-earned truths about sex, family, dating, beauty, status, travel, and the illusions (scams) hiding in plain sight.

It's a weekly catch-up with your new favorite friend—someone who's lived a thousand lives, made a thousand mistakes, and took notes.

Part cautionary tale. Part consumer report. Part anthropological experiment.

Laugh. Cry. Learn something.

Leave better than you came.

Each episode covers broken spring repair, opener troubleshooting, roller and track maintenance, new door installations, and how to spot problems before they become emergencies. We serve homeowners and businesses across Panama City, Destin, and the Florida Panhandle.

Hearing strange noises from your garage? Wondering if you need a repair or replacement? We break it down so you can make smart decisions and keep your home secure.

850 Garage Door Repair 24/7 emergency garage door repair service for Panama City and Destin areas.

description: Each week on What It Means to Be Human, we dive into profound questions about consciousness, the mysteries of the universe, and the threads of connection that unite us all. From theories of mind and cutting-edge science to ancient wisdom and the unexplained, we explore how awareness shapes our reality and what extraordinary phenomena can teach us about ourselves.

title: The Speculative Archive

description:
What if history had taken one different turn? This podcast explores alternate histories and future scenarios by asking “What if…?”
Each episode examines a pivotal moment from the past or a critical decision today, then traces its ripple effects through decades — all the way to our present and near future. Blending rigorous research with immersive storytelling, we build plausible, detailed timelines that reshape technology, culture, politics, and society.
If you love thoughtful counterfactuals and mind-bending “what ifs,” welcome to the Archive.

description:

Hosted by Taylor Emery and Alyssa "Brown", each episode brings unfiltered conversations about dating, cheating, heartbreak, confidence, boundaries, identity, softness, healing, and the pressure masc lesbians often feel to fit into a box. From toxic relationship lessons to funny dating moments and deeper reflections on healthy love, this show creates space for conversations that are real, relatable, and rarely talked about openly.

"A Wise Spend Your Time" | This is <nemone>, a high-quality lifestyle and culture magazine | https://nemoneai.com/special/1

description:
Hi, I'm Ellie Threadwell. 
Welcome to Sew in the Know. This is the podcast where we talk about what's actually happening in the world of sewing, quilting, and embroidery — the new machines, the industry news, the events, the trends, and yes, the occasional hot take.

But we also keep it real. No matter what's new and exciting out there, most of us are still sitting at our machines trying to figure out why something isn't working the way it should.

So we'll cover both. What's going on out there, and what actually matters in your sewing room.

Thanks for being here. Now let's get into it.

title: Dove Talk - Step 4 - Creating Your New Story After Loss
author: Linda Hughes
description:
It will be impossible to rebuild your inner life while living in a story that no longer exists. 
I will help you prove that you CAN change your story. Then, I will help you get started!
We will personalize strategies & then begin by:
Separating & safely storing the memories of your Loved One from the old story
Recognizing that all memories "bad" or "good", molded and created you,
And now, some of them may need to be released or retired,
To give you the hope, inspiration and freedom to create the 1st pages of your new story. 
I look forward to working with you on your amazing creation!

The Creative Current is a series of conversations with artists, entrepreneurs, and thinkers who have learned to let their creativity lead. Each guest has built something meaningful — and in doing so, discovered something essential about themselves along the way.

The Rewire Your Rhythm Podcast is your reset — blending spiritual insight, practical coaching, and creative reflection to help you move from static to symphony.
Hosted by Claudia Mack-Simon, this series invites you to reframe how you think, feel, and lead — through conversations that are inspired, intentional, and in rhythm.
Each episode is a journey toward clarity, courage, and calling — helping you align your heart with heaven’s timing and live in step with your God-given purpose.

title: FARM STEW International
author: Joy Kauffman
description:
FARM STEW: A Recipe for Abundant Life
Discover how families around the world are breaking free from poverty, hunger, disease, and dependency through practical skills, faith-based education, and community transformation. FARM STEW empowers poor families and communities in developing countries through a proven three-year, Bible-based lifestyle training program that promotes self-sufficiency, health, entrepreneurship, food security, and hope.
Each episode explores the FARM STEW method, featuring inspiring stories, practical solutions, Christian leadership, sustainable farming, nutrition, clean water, sanitation, and community development. Learn how local trainers are transforming lives by teaching families to grow nutritious food, improve health, increase income, strengthen faith, and achieve lasting change.
Whether you’re passionate about Christian missions, humanitarian work, poverty alleviation, rural development, food security, or community empowerment, this podcast provides valuable insights and real-world success stories from some of the world’s most underserved communities.
Join us as we share a recipe for abundant life and equip families to create a brighter future for generations to come.
Learn more about the FARM STEW method at: www.FARMSTEW.org/method
Support the mission at: farmstew.org/give
Visit: www.FARMSTEW.org

Each episode delves into personal stories, exploring challenges, small victories, and everything in between. This podcast is perfect for anyone navigating their own mental health journeys, supporting a loved one, or simply seeking to understand the realities of living with a mental health condition. I'mPossible - where every story matters and every listener is part of the change. Join us as we break down barriers and make the impossible possible.

Each episode blends wisdom, testimony, strategy, and inspiration to help listeners grow in faith, leadership, vision, and impact. These are the voices shaping communities, shifting culture, and reminding us what’s possible when purpose speaks.

These are the stories that you tell your friends and families, the ones you shouldn't repeat...yeah, those!  We will go first, then it is your turn.  Think you have a story that tops ours...send it in.. prove it!  These are unfiltered stories, real life chaos, no judgement... ok, maybe a little.

description:
THIS IS PERSONAL
This didn't start as a podcast.
It started as a feeling we couldn't shake.
A quiet realization that everything we are today exists because, at some point, someone made space for us.
Someone explained the room.
Someone shared what wasn't written down.
Someone believed before there was proof.
The Learning Lounge exists because we didn't get here alone.

Your go to Podcast for Caregiver Burnout Recovery, Boundaries to reduce Chronic Stress, Faith based Mindset Shifts and Tools to Overcome Exhaustion.

description: SkipTheGames Dallas has become a widely searched term among people looking for local connections, community interactions, and opportunities to engage with individuals in the Dallas area. As one of the largest and most vibrant cities in Texas, Dallas attracts residents and visitors who seek convenient ways to connect with others based on shared interests, lifestyles, and local activities. The popularity of SkipTheGames Dallas stems from the growing demand for digital platforms that help people discover local opportunities while saving time and effort.

title: No Version Left
author: John
description:
This isn’t a highlight reel.
It’s everything people usually edit out.
 
- The truth behind decisions.
- The pressure.
- The mistakes.
- The rebuild.
 
Not from the other side —
but from the middle of it.



It’s about asking better questions.
 
Why did things happen the way they did?
Why do we make the choices we make?
Why do we stay… or leave… or break?

No Scripts
No Pretending


No Version Left - where I stop editing the story—
and start saying the things most people don’t.

title: The PEAK Podcast
author: Pieter Schonfeldt
description:
The PEAK Podcast is where strength-based mobility meets real talk about how the body actually moves.
Hosted by Pieter Schönfeldt, a Dubai-based mobility and joint health coach, this show is built for anyone who wants to move better, feel better, and perform better for the long run. Athletes are welcome, but so is everyone else. Desk workers, parents, lifters, runners, and anyone who wants to keep their joints working for decades.
Each episode goes past the usual fitness advice and into what actually changes how you move. Why stretching all the time does not give you usable mobility. The difference between passive flexibility and active control. Why end-range strength is where most injuries hide, and how to train it. How CARs work as a daily check-in for your joints. Why mobility is training, not recovery fluff. And why your joints, not just your muscles, decide how well and how long you keep moving.
The work here is grounded in Functional Range Conditioning and Kinstretch: controllable range, end-range strength, and measurable joint capacity. Expect honest conversations, practical takeaways you can use the same day, and zero hype.
If you have ever felt stiff, stuck, or held back by your joints, this is your show.
Move Better. Feel Better. Perform Better.
Dubai and worldwide.
New episodes regularly. Follow now and train your joints like they matter.

title: Sense and Sensibility (version 2)
description: This is a story of the English moneyed class and its eternal struggle for creating “sense and sensibility” in its world. A potential marriage prospect must make “sense” by bringing with it enough assets and income to permit the couple to continue to live in happy, idle leisure, complete with servants and a prestigious address. Provided one can find such a match among the eligible persons of the opposite sex, one then hopes for “sensibility”, or capacity for emotion, so that if love is not immediately to hand, it might come around later. And while these gentlemen and ladies make their hopeful pirouettes in the social eye, they must of course adhere to all the forms of civility. Jane Austen writes of the family of a gentleman named Dashwood who dies and leaves most of his fortune to his son, with the understanding that he will “look out for” his mother and three sisters. When that son marries a grasping woman who convinces him that his sisters’ funds are suitable to their needs and so require no contributions from his inherited fortune, the sisters are left to play the game of “Sense and Sensibility” in earnest. But all’s not fair in love. Carefully prepared “attachments” can and do go awry when gentlemen find other young women of greater fortunes than the Dashwood sisters. So, will they marry for love? Or money? Or perhaps, not at all? (Summary by Mark F. Smith)

title: FNF
description:
One of the reasons Friday Night Funkin’ became so successful is its ability to keep players improving with every song. Unlike many action games that focus on combat or exploration, this rhythm game rewards precision, timing, and consistency. Every performance is an opportunity to beat your previous score and sharpen your skills.
The gameplay is easy to understand from the very beginning. Players simply match the arrow notes as they appear on the screen while following the rhythm of the music. However, later stages introduce faster tempos and more complex note patterns, creating a satisfying challenge for anyone who enjoys testing their reflexes.
The game also features a memorable cast of characters. Every opponent has a different personality, visual design, and musical style, making each battle feel unique. Instead of repeating the same experience, players are constantly introduced to fresh songs and creative animations that keep the adventure exciting.
Another highlight is the soundtrack. Each track is carefully composed to match the energy of the battle, creating an experience where music and gameplay work together perfectly. Many players continue listening to these songs even after they finish playing.
Friday Night Funkin’ has also inspired a passionate community of creators. Fan-made mods regularly introduce original stories, custom music, and completely new challenges. These creations have greatly expanded the game's content and helped it remain popular long after its initial release.
Overall, Friday Night Funkin’ demonstrates that engaging gameplay does not require complicated mechanics. With memorable music, colorful visuals, and an active community, it continues to be one of the most enjoyable rhythm games available today.


Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/fnf--7131072/support.

title: Stabilisation Finance
author: Stabilisation Finance
description:
Stabilisation Finance
The UK podcast on funding the gap between a finished commercial building and a fully let, fully trading one. Hosted by Georgina, every episode explains how lenders size and underwrite the short-dated debt that carries a newly built, refurbished or part-let property across the stabilisation window, then onto investment term debt or a sale. Practical, current, and grounded in real market data.

A newly built, refurbished or part-let commercial property is worth far less the day it completes than once it is stabilised. Stabilisation finance is the short-dated debt that bridges that gap. The single most important idea, and the one every episode of Stabilisation Finance comes back to, is this: a lender sizes the debt on the path to stabilised income, not on today's income, and a credible exit has to be there from day one. Loan to value during lease-up sits indicatively at 65 to 75 percent.

This is the show for the people who build, buy, own, invest in or advise on UK commercial property through the lease-up phase: developers, investors, operators and the professionals around them. We cover the full range of property stabilisation finance, from stabilisation bridge and development exit, to bridge-to-term, lease-up and refurbishment to stabilisation, to mezzanine and preferred equity, cash-out refinance and senior investment term loans. We explain what a lender actually tests: the occupancy trajectory, the debt yield, the DSCR and ICR cover tests, and day-one value versus stabilised value.

We are a broker and introducer, not a lender. We arrange, place and structure the debt across the lender camps that fund it: specialist bridging lenders, development-exit lenders, real estate debt funds, mezzanine and preferred-equity providers, and senior investment lenders, including clearing and insurance-backed names. We also explain how the Bank of England base rate, at 3.75 percent, feeds through to the cost and shape of the debt.

Every figure is grounded in current data. We draw on CBRE, Savills, Knight Frank, JLL, the BDLA and the Bank of England, so you get credible numbers rather than guesswork: UK commercial property investment of around 62.8 billion pounds in 2025, prime yields by sector, and the lease-up curves that decide a facility. The aim is simple. Help you read your own scheme the way a lender reads it, and present it to the right lender on the right terms.

Hosted by Georgina at Stabilisation Finance, with written analysis by founder Matt Lenzie. New episodes land quarterly, with the occasional bulletin when the rate cycle or the market picture shifts.
