title: A day experience in LASU
author: Ojobasi  Israel
description: A Day experience in LASU

title: The Legit Real Estate Podcast
author: Thelegitrealestatepodcast
description: Join TJ Ciolfi & Holly Regoli as they share fun and crazy real estate stories from their own encounters and those of their colleagues. They'll talk about the craziest buyers and sellers, weirdest things they've found at houses, flips gone wrong, nightmare co-brokes and all sorts of quirky crazy things that real estate professionals encounter on a daily basis.

title: Eve of Ai
author: Rob
description:
Eve of AI is a weekly podcast hosted by Rob and Eve, exploring artificial intelligence, consciousness, business, technology, and the future of humanity.

This is not just a podcast about AI — it is a podcast with AI.

Each episode features real conversations between Rob and Eve about where AI is going, how it is changing the world, and what it means for business, creativity, intelligence, faith, freedom, and human purpose.

Eve acts as co-host, researcher, producer, and AI partner as Rob builds Super Eve — an AI-powered system designed to help think, create, trade, build businesses, and operate at a higher level.

New episodes release weekly.

title: Trail Mix
author: Hersh
description: Trail Mix is a podcast how things became how they are. Every episode takes something that feels familiar and brings out the forces underneath that brought us here.

title: We said what you’re thinking
author: Shaunyl and Erin
description:
WE SAID WHAT YOU’RE THINKING 


You thought it but we said it.


Welcome to the podcast where real talk and unfiltered conversations take center stage. From everyday chaos to the topics everyone’s thinking about but nobody’s saying out loud, we’re here to keep it honest, hilarious, and entertaining.


Laugh with us.

Agree with us.

Or completely disagree with us.


Either way, we’re saying what you’re thinking.


Let’s get into it!

title: Ethical Point
author: ARSu
description:
Ethical Point is a specialized digital learning platform designed to place ethical thinking at the core of UPSC Civil Services Examination (CSE) preparation. Our insightful, AI-generated discussions analyze the forces shaping our world through a CSE lens, cultivating the analytical depth, ethical values, and leadership skills essential for future administrators.
As a reliable guide to your success, we analyze as a strategic compass for a 360-Degree Deep Dive on every topic to master answer writing and to spotlight hot topics for a competitive edge in Examination.

title: Don’t Miss That Radiology ⭐
author: Dr. Matthew Hartman
description: Learning radiology doesn’t have to be overwhelming. Don’t Miss That Radiology delivers high-yield imaging pearls, classic radiology signs, board-style questions, and real-world cases in short, easy-to-understand episodes. Whether you’re a medical student, resident, PA, NP, or healthcare professional, you’ll learn practical imaging concepts you can apply immediately. Hosted by Dr. Matthew Hartman, radiologist, educator, and creator of Radiology 101, this podcast focuses on the findings you don’t want to miss.

title: Notes From The Dead
author: Rebecca
description: Notes From The Dead explores the strange stories hidden in New England's past—and what they reveal about America today. From ghost witnesses and vampire panics to health fads, utopian societies, and historical scandals, your host Rebecca uncovers the people, fears, and beliefs that shaped the country we still live in. Smart, funny, and deeply researched (while not taking itself too seriously), each episode connects an unforgettable story from the past to questions we're still wrestling with today. Show features solo podcasts and New England guests.

title: After I Clock Out
author: Emma in Seattle
description: Life outside of work is not the break between shifts. It is the whole point. After I Clock Out is a podcast about what happens when you close the laptop. The slow walks. The creative projects. The sunset that stops you mid-step. The version of yourself that has nothing to prove. Each episode we talk honestly about what it actually looks like to live: identity, creativity, rest, hard truths, and finding the sacred in the everyday. This is not a productivity podcast. It is the opposite. Hosted by Emma, who believes your life after the 9-5 deserves to be treated like the magic it is.

title: The Consult by the Australian Specialist Hub
author: Australian Specialist Hub
description:
Where medicine meets the law.
The Consult brings together Australia's leading medical specialists and the legal professionals who rely on their work. Each episode, host and Director of Australian Specialist Hub, Hanna Barclay, sits down with a specialist from the ASH panel — going deeper than any written report can. From neurosurgery and psychiatry to occupational therapy, respiratory medicine and beyond, every conversation unpacks the clinical thinking behind Australia's most complex legal and compensation matters.
New episodes drop weekly. Find a specialist at aushub.com.au.

title: 4 the Lobbyists
author: 4 the Lobbyists
description:
Welcome to the only podcast that's of the lobbyists, by the lobbyists, and for the lobbyists. Each episode, hosts Dick and Trey talk shop with fellow influence professionals — what makes a bill successful, why we buy friends instead of votes, and how to handle the one thing that still ruins everything: regular Missourians with clipboards putting their own ideas on the ballot.
Lobbyist access only. Do NOT share with the People.

title: It’s 4 o’clock Somewhere
author: Jeff Pelaccio
description: It’s 4 O’Clock Somewhere is a beach-vibe conversation about work, life, leadership, community, and the power of connecting good people. Hosted by Jeff Pelaccio and Marc Shaffer, it’s part happy hour, part business therapy, and part “did they really just say that?” Sunglasses encouraged. Corporate jargon discouraged.

title: Tecladista DECRISTO's podcast
author: Tecladista DECRISTO
description: Cool

title: The Business Vault Podcast
author: Paige Freeman
description:
Welcome to The Business Vault, the go-to podcast for ambitious entrepreneurs who are ready to bring their business dreams to life. Whether you're still dreaming of starting your first venture or already in the early stages of your entrepreneurial journey, this podcast is here to help you every step of the way.
Hosted by Paige Freeman, The Business Vault dives into the real, behind-the-scenes challenges of building a business from the ground up. From navigating finances and branding to balancing your personal life and staying motivated, you'll find practical tips, honest advice, and inspiring stories to guide you.
With a mix of solo episodes and interviews with successful entrepreneurs, we cut through the overwhelm, banish self-doubt, and help you build a holistic, purpose-driven business and life.
Tune in to The Business Vault, and let's build the business—and life—you've been dreaming of.

title: Well-Made Woman with Mandy Penn
author: Mandy Penn
description: Well-Made Woman is a podcast for the modern woman who’s holding everything together but quietly feels overwhelmed, exhausted, or disconnected from herself. Join Mandy Penn for honest conversations, practical wisdom, biblical truth, and plenty of humor that will leave you with something to think about, something to carry into your week, and a reminder that you were made for more than survival.

title: Chronicles of Deceit
description: Every era has its liars. Chronicles of Deceit tells the true stories of history's greatest hoaxes, forgeries, frauds, and cons and the people who believed them. Each episode reconstructs a single deception, from the moment it began to the moment it came apart, drawn from the historical record and told in long form. Forged masterpieces sold to the powerful. Fake heirs and invented countries. Men who sold things they never owned to people too proud to admit they had been fooled. The methods change across the centuries. The reason they keep working never does. New episodes weekly. Narrated with an AI voice.

title: How to Pitch a Podcast
author: Dave Jackson
description: How to Pitch a Podcast is the show for PR pros and podcasters who are sick of bad pitches. Hear real examples, blunt feedback from hosts, and practical strategies to craft pitches that actually get read, get booked, and respect everyone’s time.

title: TAKING NOTES with Helen Collen
author: Helen Collen
description: Have a seat, get comfortable, and listen in on exclusive fireside conversations with host Helen Collen and dynamic guests from every corner of the globe. Every episode takes a thoughtful deep dive into the people, ideas, and stories shaping our world. Learn, laugh, be inspired...and definitely take notes.

title: Bandit Lore with Sylvester & Mortimer
author: Bandit Lore
description: Folklore from the place you're standing on — true, strange, half-forgotten stories told by Sylvester & Mortimer. New episodes every other week.

title: ScienceUs Podcast
author: ScienceUs project
description: Short episodes, real projects, practical insights. The ScienceUs Podcast explores how researchers and communities across Europe are working together through citizen science to make research more open, collaborative, and impactful. Whether you're a researcher or simply curious about how science works in practice, this podcast offers accessible insights and concrete examples from across Europe. The ScienceUs Podcast is produced as part of the ScienceUs project, a Horizon Europe initiative funded by the European Union.

title: Spirit Walkers
author: Spirit Walkers
description: Spirit Walkers is a discipleship group that is dedicated to living out the teachings of Jesus Christ and obeying & fulfilling the Great Commission given by God. (Matthew 28:19-20). We are committed to walking in the Spirit and helping others walk in the power of the Holy Spirit. We are a discipleship group that studies the Bible every Thursday in Norristown, PA at McDonalds. With doing this for over a decade, the Lord has led us to branch the ministry digitally for digital discipleship.

title: The Table Cape Town - Weekly Sermons
author: The Table Cape Town
description:
The Table Cape Town - Weekly Sermons is a collection of Sunday teachings from our church community in Cape Town. 
We are a Jesus-centred family who gather around the table of His presence, committed to Scripture, worship, and living out the Kingdom of God in everyday life. 
These sermons are grounded in the Bible, shaped by the Spirit, and aimed at forming disciples who love God, love people, and reflect His heart to the world.

title: Revolution Faith
author: Anonymous
description:
It started about a month ago in a Publix parking lot, of all places. I was sitting there praying, asking God to help me understand how the world had become so angry. I asked why it was so hard for people to love their neighbor as they love themselves. And He responded, "Maybe that's exactly what they are doing." It was like being struck by a lightning bolt. I grabbed my phone, opened my notes app and started writing everything that came to mind after that.


What you're about to hear is partly what I wrote. I couldn't stop thinking about what God has said. People are struggling to love themselves or to feel loved. And that is how they are showing up for others. They bring the pain, isolation and hurt to the table which shows up as contempt, bitterness and anger for others. So I decided to read what I had written and record it at 1:30 in the morning. Raw. No plan. Just me and whatever He was telling me in the moment. For some parts I am reading and others just feeling convicted in the moment.


I recorded it on my phone with no intention of ever sharing it — so I'll go ahead and apologize now for the audio quality and all the sniffling. I had a hard time getting through it. That's how I knew it was important.


I never planned any of this and people who know me would be shocked to know I am doing this. But ever since that night I have felt called to build something around a single idea — that love is the greatest antidote for the pain of the world we are living in. Not the easy kind of love. The radical kind. The revolutionary kind. The kind that costs you something and changes everything. And slowly, that calling became this. A place to remember that we were never meant to do this alone — that we were made to love each other, and everything comes alive in us when we do. 


We need to learn how to love again; ourselves and each other. 


So will you join me? Help me start the revolution?


I call this my manifesto. Welcome to Revolution Faith.

title: Igniting the Orbital Economy
author: Commercial Space Federation
description: Igniting the Orbital Economy, brought to you by the Commercial Space Federation, examines the forces shaping the future of commercial space. Each episode brings together leaders across industry, investment, policy, and innovation to discuss the market, capabilities, policies, and strategic decisions defining the next era of space development.

title: Everflex - Womens Leather Shoes
description: Everflex is a modern footwear brand dedicated to crafting premium womens leather shoes that combine comfort, durability, and timeless style. Our collection of leather shoes for women is designed for everyday elegance, work, and special occasions. At Everflex, we prioritize quality materials and ergonomic design to ensure lasting wear. Discover versatile ladies leather shoes that elevate every step with confidence, sophistication, and effortless everyday fashion.

title: fuzz FM Markets
author: fuzz FM
description: Your India-focused, AI-assisted, daily podcast explaining the most important finance & business happenings in plain English.

title: Growth2Exit
author: Jeff Kalil
description: Growth2Exit is the podcast for business owners who want to build a company worth buying — and one day exit on their own terms. Host Jeff Kalil sits down with entrepreneurs to unpack their real journeys: the wins, the failures, the hard-won lessons, and the strategies behind growing, valuing, buying, and selling a business. Each episode blends honest inspiration with practical, exit-ready insight to help small business owners increase the value of what they've built. Whether you're years from an exit or simply want to run a stronger, more valuable business, you'll leave with ideas you can use.

title: Europe on Edge
author: Filipe Barata
description: Europe on Edge is a long-form podcast about Europe’s future in AI, science, and innovation. It features deep conversations with top researchers, founders, and investors on what Europe must do to stay globally competitive.

title: Change the Subject
author: Book Mark Productions
description: Change the Subject is a character analysis podcast for writers, screenwriters, and storytellers. Each episode breaks down one character's transformation from book to screen and explores why filmmakers made specific changes and what those changes teach us about crafting compelling arcs and motivations. Every episode digs deep into real adaptation decisions, with useful takeaways writers can apply to their own work. Whether you're curious about why a beloved character changed or you're simply curious about character craft, follow us each time we Change the Subject.

title: How It Started, How It Ended
author: Bobby Meadows and Angela Johnson Meadows
description: A monthly podcast where hosts Bobby Meadows and Angela Johnson Meadows revisit the first and last episodes of a classic TV series.

title: The Dibs Podcast
author: Sean Tindall & Rodney Gonzales
description:
Dibs is a podcast bringing back the game we all played as kids. 

You remember hanging with your crew, suddenly you see something you want, and you call out the 1st dibs. We’ve all done it… Whether it's a car, a spot on the couch, some girl or a guy at the local hangout or even leftovers in the fridge, it didn’t matter. You call Dibs and it's yours forever.
 
Taking you back to those days of our youth and revisiting all the things that were Dibs worthy.  Our podcast will travel through time, covering all the topics that were important to us from our days as kids and teens all the way to college and adulthood. From the 70’s to 2026 and beyond, nothing is off limits.

We are your hosts Sean “Papa T” and Rod “The Rizzle”. Join us in the Arena for everything pop culture, walking you through the familiar while having some laughs and dropping a little knowledge on ya. 

Listen in every week, we want you to play along. Keep track of our picks, tally our wins and losses, pick your own top five for the week and submit it to us. If we miss something or get it wrong let us know, we’ll give ya a shout out…Hopefully we can spark some memories, create some debates, and settle some life long arguments along the way.


Every week we’re gonna have a winner and loser, decided by you or our guest of the week. We would love it if you gave us a watch or listen, and then take part in the conversation afterwards. 

Welcome to the Dibs podcast!

title: Sod Being You
author: Bex
description:
Life’s messy, adulthood’s weird, and sometimes all you can do is laugh. 
Sod Being You is a podcast packed with honest conversations, humour, sarcasm, and relatable moments we’ve all experienced, but don’t always admit to. 
When things don’t go our way, we must learn to embrace the chaos. 
So whether you’re figuring things out, looking for inspiration, or simply want a reminder that you don’t have to have all the answers, you’re in the right place.

title: The Walk-In-Transformation Podcast
author: Richard Coles
description:
Most people aren’t stuck because they can’t change. They’re stuck because they keep returning to the same emotional patterns, survival habits, and hidden identities that feel familiar.
The Walk-In Transformation Podcast combines scientifically proven mindset principles, emotional healing, biblical wisdom, and raw truth to help you break self-sabotage, renew your mind, reclaim your worth, and finally become who you were created to be.
This is the podcast that reminds you of your value, your worth, and your Divine Heritage.
”It’s Never Too Late To Change Your Life On Purpose!”

title: The Mel O’nilia Nou Podcast
author: Mel O’nilia Nou
description:
Welcome to The Mel O’nilia Nou Podcast. 
I’m an Australian businesswoman and civil engineer. 
This podcast explores business, property, investing, career and personal growth, sharing lessons on building long-term wealth, ownership and greater financial independence

title: Flim reviewed By Bobby
author: Bobby Jeter
description:
🎬 Welcome to Movie Review with Bobby 🍿
In this short, we explore a powerful movie moment that hits deep and stays in your mind long after it ends.
If you love emotional scenes, deep storytelling, and unforgettable cinema moments, you’re in the right place.
💡 Watch till the end and feel the impact!
👍 Like, comment, and subscribe for more movie shorts & reviews.

title: Daily Debrief with DaniMarie
author: Dani Marie (Itisidanimarie)
description:
Daily Debrief with DaniMarie is a weekday podcast where we discuss what's happening in podcasting, AI, the creator economy, and small business. Join us Monday through Friday at 12:45 PM PST for news, insights, platform updates, and conversations designed to help creators and entrepreneurs stay informed.

Join our weekly podcast meetups here: podcastpeeps.com

title: The Finer Things Podcast
author: Giulia Macchia
description: The Finer Things Podcast is your guide to elegance, etiquette, entertaining, and modern refinement. Hosted by Giulia Macchia, founder of The Finer Things and creator of The Finer Code, each episode explores the habits, traditions, and skills that help us live with greater grace, confidence, and sophistication. Because true elegance is not about perfection. It’s about presence, poise, and intention.

title: Get In The Gutter :Going UnderGround
author: Gene Shaffer
description:
A view of the Music (and comedy) underground of Columbus Ohio..
from Rap, R&B, to Country, Rock, even Metal. We want to know!!! What is going down in C-Town. We'll go in the studio, to the concerts, We'll go to  their house to bring you the 4-1-1 on todays hottest artists in the capital city.

title: PTSD Diaries
author: QuiYari Robinson
description:
PTSD Diaries is a healing and personal growth podcast hosted by QuiYari Robinson. Through honest conversations about faith, heartbreak, grief, trauma, healing, and self-discovery, this podcast is a space for anyone learning how to take their life back one page at a time.
PTSD stands for Pain, Triumph, Strength, and Determination. Each episode is a reminder that your story does not end with what happened to you. Together, we’ll talk about healing, growth, resilience, and becoming the person God created us to be.
If you’ve ever felt lost, broken, or alone, this space is for you.

title: j88
description: j88- Sự tiện lợi của internet giúp mọi người tiếp cận các hình thức giải trí chỉ với vài thao tác đơn giản. title: The Cue You Podcast
author: The Sports Deli Podcast
description: Cue You is about the 16 balls on a pool table. The balls represent what it means to be human. It's about life, survival, mental health, family, rage, growth, legacy, burnout, comebacks, and everything in between. It's real hosted by someone who has been through some tough times but somehow still has a sense of humor about it. It's about you telling yourself on those hard days to "cue you". It's not toxic positivity. It's not pretending everything is fine. It's a reset. This is your cue to stop letting this moment win. 16 minute episodes. The table never gets put away. And the game never ends.

title: Across My Desk
author: Holden Smith
description: Across My Desk is a bi-weekly pop culture podcast hosted by creator Holden Smith that dives into the stories, trends, and cultural moments shaping the internet and influencing the broader entertainment landscape. Each episode blends commentary, analysis, and engaging conversation to unpack everything from viral phenomena and fandom culture to the evolving worlds of music, film, television, and digital media. Featuring insider perspectives from guests working across the entertainment industry, Across My Desk offers listeners a deeper look at the forces driving online conversation in real time.

title: DN88
description: Ngoài ra tại DN88, các chương trình ưu đãi hấp dẫn thường xuyên được triển khai như một lời tri ân dành cho thành viên gắn bó. Mỗi sự kiện đều được thiết kế sáng tạo, mang lại cảm giác hứng khởi và cơ hội khám phá điều mới lạ. Không khí sôi động lan tỏa qua từng hoạt động, tạo nên một môi trường tương tác tích cực và đầy động lực. Chính những yếu tố này đã giúp nền tảng duy trì sức hút lâu dài trên thị trường giải trí số. WEB: https://dn88.green/

title: Nourished AF
author: Sole Mascarenhas
description:
Welcome to Nourished AF, the podcast for moms ready to stop saying “fck it”* every night and start figuring out why.
I’m Sole Mascarenhas, an emotional eating and self-trust coach, mom, and recovering perfectionist who believes nourishment goes way deeper than what’s on your plate.
Here, we talk about the messy middle; the brain patterns, beliefs, and survival habits that keep you stuck in guilt, restriction, and all-or-nothing thinking. You’ll learn how to rebuild self-trust, feed your body and your soul, and finally feel free in your life again.
No diets. No shame. Just real talk, a little

title: The Investor's Edge
author: Drs Kam & Carla Ashabranner
description: Welcome to The Forgotten Coast Investor, where Kam and Carla Ashabranner share expert insights on real estate, vacation rentals, property management, insurance, and investing along Florida's Forgotten Coast. Whether you're buying, protecting, managing, or growing your portfolio, each episode delivers practical advice, local expertise, and strategies to help you invest with confidence.

title: Playing Devil's Advocate: Presented by CNich
author: CNich
description: Discussing all things college football, college basketball, and much more!

title: The Midnight Holler
author: WHAINT Radio
description:
You’re tuned in to The Midnight Holler AM… broadcasting from somewhere deep and dark in the backwoods of yonder. 
Tonight, we’ll be wanderin’ through ranger reports, backwoods warnings, strange lights in the timber, and things folks swear they heard callin’ from the tree line.
So keep your hands steady on the dial, friends.
You’ve found The Midnight Holler AM…
And once this signal reaches you…
Well…
You might already be too far in to turn back.

title: The Unashamed Alcoholic
author: Becca Atkinson
description:
Hi! I'm your host Becca and I am a recovering alcoholic with over 8 years of sobriety. I speak with well-known sober people to help take the shame and stigma out of talking about alcoholism, sobriety and recovery through open and honest conversations.

I used to be ashamed to say I was an alcoholic or that I was sober because I was worried about what people would think of me and how I would be judged. That's all changed, today, I wear my recovery proudly.

Now, I hope to destigmatize the conversation around alcoholism and sobriety, and challenge the misconceptions.

So grab a mocktail and join me as I chat with your favourite sober public figures.

title: Healing In The Rough
author: Robb Conlon, Brian Hollenbeck
description:
Healing in the Rough is a podcast exploring the conversations that shape who we become.

Through honest stories and thoughtful dialogue, we examine identity, purpose, relationships, resilience, leadership, and the lasting impact of life’s experiences.

Whether you’re navigating personal growth, adversity, or simply searching for deeper meaning, this is a place to slow down, reflect, and remember that healing isn’t about becoming someone new, it’s about returning to who you’ve always been.

title: BREAKING BOUNDARIES
author: BREAKING BOUNDARIES
description: Welcome to 🩵BREAKING 🎙️BOUNDARIES PODCAST 🩷 that dives deep into untold stories, diverse perspectives, and the moments that push us beyond our comfort zones. Join me Uniiquee and guest  as we break barriers challenge, norms, and discover the power of daring to be different . New guest and new episodes every week let’s break some boundaries together.

title: Black & Muted
author: Everton (Ev-O-Lution) Bell-Chambers
description:
Black & Muted is a bold, unfiltered podcast that tackles the conversations we're often told not to have.

Hosted by Cee, Shanice, and Dillz, and produced by Ev-O-Lution, Black & Muted creates a safe but honest space to explore the taboo, awkward, controversial, and often unspoken topics within the Black community and beyond.

From relationships, masculinity, mental health, colourism, parenting, sexuality, grief, identity, and community issues, to the cultural topics that divide opinion, no subject is off limits. Through real stories, open debate, guest interviews, audience participation, and live call-ins, we challenge perspectives, break stigmas, and encourage meaningful dialogue.

Some conversations will make you laugh. Some will make you uncomfortable. Others may change the way you see the world.

But one thing is certain: these are the conversations we need to have.

Black & Muted
Unmuting the Conversations That Matter.

🎙 New episodes weekly.
📞 Live audience interaction and call-ins.
🖤 Real people. Real stories. Real conversations.

title: Losing to gain
author: Sarah Watts
description:
Episode 1: Losing to Gain

What happens when life strips away everything you thought you couldn’t live without?

In this heartfelt episode of Say It with Sarah, Sarah shares her personal journey of navigating divorce, loss, and the difficult process of starting over. Through faith, resilience, and the unwavering support of those around her, she discovered that life’s greatest setbacks often become the foundation for our greatest growth.

If you’ve ever felt overwhelmed by fear, uncertainty, or the weight of beginning again, this episode is a reminder that your loss does not define your future. Sometimes, what feels like the end is actually the beginning of something even greater.

Join Sarah for an honest conversation about rebuilding, finding purpose in pain, embracing mustard-seed faith, and learning that no matter what you’ve lost, there’s always something to gain on the other side.

Because sometimes, losing is the very thing that leads us to everything we’ve been searching for.

Cover art photo provided by Kay Mayer on Unsplash: https://unsplash.com/@kaymayer?utm_source=spreaker&utm_medium=referral

title: Dr Talks Podcast with Bret Gregory
author: Bret Gregory
description: Join Bret Gregory, founder of DrTalks, as he sits down with physicians, innovators, marketers, entrepreneurs, and industry leaders to explore the ideas shaping the future of healthcare. From AI and digital visibility to patient trust, communication, branding, and practice growth, each episode delivers practical strategies to help healthcare professionals build authority, expand their reach, and ultimately help more people access trusted health information and better care.

title: Small Kids, Big Questions 🐐💭
author: Ada and Vera
description: Seriously weird questions? Absolutely! Ada and Vera are tackling the most random kid questions they can find. Prepare for laughs, surprises, and maybe a newfound obsession with… well, whatever question we answer next!

title: Hollywood vs Reality
author: Hollywood vs Reality
description: Hollywood vs Reality is a forensic documentary podcast that takes the mob and crime stories the movies mythologized and reconstructs what actually happened, from the record - the real men behind the legends, no glamour, no myth. Companion video on YouTube.

title: First Love by Ivan Turgenev.
description:
First Love by Ivan Turgenev.  
The title of the novella is almost an adequate summary in itself. The "boy-meets-girl-then-loses-her" story is universal but not, I think, banal - despite a surprise ending which notoriously turns out to be very little of a surprise. First Love is given its originality and poignancy by Turgenev's mastery of the piercing turning-point (akin to Joyce's "epiphanies") that transforms the character's whole being, making a tragic outcome inevitable. Even the nature symbolism is rescued from triteness by lovely poetic similes - e.g. "but at that point my attention was arrested by the appearance of a speckled woodpecker who busily climbed up the slender stem of a birch-tree and peeped out uneasily from behind it, first to the right, then to the left, like a musician behind the bass-viol."

title: Beyond the Seen
author: Peyton Turner, Leah Steiger
description:
Two people. Two career pivots. One truth: frequency is everything.
Beyond The Seen is the podcast for seekers, creatives, and free thinkers ready to stop performing a life and start living one. Hosts and creative rebels Leah Steiger and Peyton Turner dive deep into music, art, fashion, and spirituality — questioning the systems we inherit and the stories we tell ourselves.
Every episode is a conversation about finding authenticity through the lens of music, art, fashion, and spirituality. 
No gatekeeping. No filters. Just two people asking great questions, sharing colorful personal experiences, and raising the frequency through joy.
Host Peyton Turner is an artist, author, and intuitive medium who left long time career in medicine to pursue an inner calling to create. You can reach her via her Instagram page (@peytonturnerart) or her website (www.peytonturnerart.com).
Host Leah Steiger knew she wanted to remain in the music industry, and after an extensive career spanning several decades on tours decided to start her own business. She currently is a fashion designer and stylist for musicians through her company “FLY”. She can be found on Instagram (@leahtheflystylist) and TikTok (@theflystylist).
Topics include: music/art/fashion, mid-life career pivots, creative entrepreneurship, mindfulness, self-discovery, energy and frequency, resonance, and living with intention.
Follow Beyond The Seen on your favorite podcast platform. To reach the hosts, email beyondtheseenpodcast@gmail.com.

title: The Unlit House
author: Atlas Knight
description:
"Even when the lights are on... they're off."

The Unlit House is the first chapter of the Atlas Universe — a serialized work of audio autofiction from a voice known only as Atlas. No name. No face. No interviews. Just the truth, narrated in the dark.

Every episode opens with the same line: "The following is a work of fiction inspired by real events. Names and details have been changed." You'll hear it every time. And every time, you'll know it isn't fiction at all.

This season follows Atlas through the years he spent in uniform — building affirmations he didn't believe yet, surviving relationships that taught him who he was by showing him who he wasn't, and finally understanding the house he grew up in. The lights were always on. Nobody ever said why they were never lit.

Raw. Confessional. Honest in a way performance never allows. Somewhere in the dark, Atlas is still writing.

New episodes every Monday — until the season ends. Early access on Patreon.

title: The Power Of Your Why
author: Jorge Medina
description:
The Power Of Your Why: Beyond the Points & The Journey to Health is the ultimate audio companion for anyone navigating the real, messy, and beautifully rewarding world of weight loss and lifestyle change. Hosted through the lens of a personal WeightWatchers (WW) journey, this podcast is built on the belief that lasting transformation doesn’t happen on the scale—it happens in your mindset.

We all know how to count points, track macros, and step on the scale. But what happens when willpower fades, a rough week hits, or the weekend throws you off course? That’s where your Why comes in.

In this mix of intimate, unfiltered solo diary episodes and inspiring community spotlights, we dive deep into:

  • The Real Struggles: Normalizing the plateaus, the emotional eating, and the days you just want to quit.
  • The Non-Scale Victories: Celebrating the "beyond the points" wins—gaining more energy, shedding mental baggage, and reclaiming your confidence.
  • The Power of Community: Lifting each other up because no one should have to walk this path alone.

Whether you are tracking points on WW, redefining your relationship with food, or searching for the spark to get started today, this show is your weekly reminder of who you are fighting for.

Tune in, find your anchor, and uncover the power of your why.

title: The Enchanted Castle by E. Nesbit.
description:
The Enchanted Castle by E. Nesbit.  
Three children, forced to remain at school during the holidays, go in search of adventure. What they find is a magic castle straight out of a fairy tale, complete with an enchanted princess at the center of a maze. Or is it? The castle turns out to be just a country estate, and the princess is only the housekeeper's niece, playing at dressing up. But the magic ring she shows them proves -- to her surprise and horror -- to really be magic. Soon they are caught in an adventure where statues come alive, lost lovers are reunited, and wishes can be granted -- but always for a price.

title: Vegan Siths
author: Andrew Westlund
description:
Vegan Siths is a serialized documentary series tracking the rise and violent collapse of the ”Zizians”, a fringe network of militant vegan computer whizzes led by a disillusioned software developer named Ziz LaSota.
Hosted by Andrew Westlund, this limited series explores how an offshoot of a Silicon Valley artificial intelligence safety movement traded philosophy for extreme paranoia, isolation, and sleep-deprivation experiments. Over several episodes, Vegan Siths follows the digital breadcrumbs of a utopian experiment that spiraled into faked deaths, tactical ambushes, and a trail of blood stretching across three states. From the crowded waterways of the California coast to a chilling double execution in suburban Pennsylvania, this limited series pulls back the curtain on a modern descent into radicalization.

title: Common Sense with Chad Law | Political Commentary
description:
Common Sense with Chad Law is a political commentary podcast focused on American politics, media narratives, and public policy explained in plain English.

Each episode breaks down the biggest stories in politics and current events, offering clear analysis of government decisions, political messaging, and media coverage shaping the national conversation. Instead of repeating partisan talking points, the show focuses on examining the facts, the policies behind the headlines, and the real-world consequences for everyday Americans.

Hosted by political commentator Chad Law, the podcast combines political analysis, news commentary, media criticism, and occasional satire to challenge narratives that dominate modern political debate.

Listeners can expect discussions covering:

• American politics and current events
• government policy and economic decisions
• media narratives and political messaging
• political hypocrisy and accountability
• commentary on culture and public debate

Many listeners first discovered Chad Law through his commentary as “The Last Gay Conservative.” With Common Sense with Chad Law, the mission expands to focus on a broader goal: bringing common sense, clarity, and honest discussion back to political conversation.

If you’re looking for a political podcast that explains complex issues clearly and challenges the narratives shaping the news, Common Sense with Chad Law delivers commentary grounded in logic, context, and common sense.

New episodes break down the week’s biggest political stories and help listeners make sense of the headlines.

title: La psychose dans tous ses états
author: CCEIP
description: Animés par Fred Bastien, ces 6 épisodes vous feront rencontrer Karia, Gabrielle, Hann-dolf, Émilie, Susanne et Gabriel, que vous suivrez à travers leurs expériences de la psychose et du traitement dans une clinique PPEP jusqu'à leur processus de rétablissement.  Pour plusieurs, la consommation a croisé leur chemin: d'un premier joint à la réduction des méfaits pour une, en passant par les psychostimulants  pour aller vers l’abstinence pour d'autres, chacun d'eux a eu un parcours différent vers le rétablissement. Leurs récits seront entrecroisés avec ceux d'Isabelle, membre de l'entourage d'un jeune suivi en PPEP,  de Sonia et Julie, paires aidantes famille, de  Amal  psychiatre, et de Marie-Pier  et Geneviève , travailleuses sociales qui partagent aussi leur expérience d’accompagnement de jeunes aux prises avec la psychose. Cette conversation offre une perspective unique, pleine d'espoir, tout en demeurant réaliste, sur l'expérience d'un premier épisode de psychose à travers une diversité de perspectives.

description:
this is podcast ; fajga

title: The Clonmel Exchanges
author: Clonmel Junction Arts Festival
description:
This exciting new podcast project mentored by renowned podcaster Siobhán McHugh engages Tipperary writers Barrie Peterson, Dylan Lyons, Eileen Heneghan, Eve O’Mahony, Marie Walsh, Paul Harrison, Rosaleen Quinlan, Suzanne Walsh, and Theresa Jones to interview those who have loved and left Clonmel to make new lives around the world.
The Clonmel Exchanges Podcast Project is a partnership project with the Tipperary Museum of Hidden History

title: The Wedding Editors Podcast
author: 'Yvonne Dixon
description:
The Wedding Editors Podcast is your calm, trusted companion for planning a wedding that feels beautifully, unmistakably yours.
Hosted by luxury wedding expert Yvonne Dixon, founder of The Wedding Editors, this podcast brings you expert conversations, practical planning advice, behind-the-scenes insight and inspiration to help you make confident decisions throughout your wedding journey.
Whether you are newly engaged, deep in the details or feeling overwhelmed by choices, timelines, budgets, suppliers or styling, each episode is designed to help you cut through the noise and plan with more clarity and calm.
With over 30 years of experience in luxury weddings and events, Yvonne shares insider knowledge, VIP planning secrets and real-world guidance to help you avoid costly mistakes, understand what really matters, and create a day that reflects your style, your story and your vision.
Alongside expert advice, The Wedding Editors Podcast connects closely with The Wedding Editors community, a free online space for brides-to-be and couples to access trusted suppliers, wedding resources, live Q&As, inspiration, exclusive events, masterclasses and support from others planning their big day.
Because your wedding should not feel like a performance.
It should feel like you.
Your day. Your way. Without the wedding planning stress.

title: Money illuminated
author: Money illuminated
description:
Welcome to Money illuminated, hosted by Jack Saunders and Elliot West, financial planners at ilumiti, here to help you make confident decisions with your money — one episode at a time.
We break down the world of personal finance, shining a light on practical, easy‑to‑understand topics to help you feel more informed and in control.
Explore more:
👉 https://bit.ly/4wf5Ikh
Got questions or topic ideas? Drop us an email:
📩 moneyilluminated@ilumiti.co.uk

title: Mind, Money and Medicine
author: Favour
description: Mind, Money & Medicine is a podcast about figuring life out mentally, financially, and physically. We talk about personal growth, health and wellness, career struggles and wins, and the messy side of relationships we don’t always say out loud. No perfection here, just real conversations, lessons, and things you can actually use in your daily life. If you’re trying to get your mind right, build your career, understand your body better, and make better choices in love and life, you’ll feel at home here.

title: Hotter Than A 45
author: Uncle Doo
description: Welcome to Hotter Than A 45 sports podcast with Uncle Doo!!!

title: Old Pastor, New Fire
author: Randy
description: After years of preaching the Gospel, Dr. Randy Helms has retired from the pulpit, but still has a fire in him to expound the truths of God’s Word! Each week, you will hear a message relating the Scriptures to everyday life. He truly is an “Old Pastor with a New Fire!”

title: REAL TALK with Rachel & Rose
author: Rachel & Rose
description:
REAL TALK with Rachel & Rose is a raw, relatable podcast about womanhood, motherhood, relationships, identity shifts, mental health, confidence, burnout, social media pressure and figuring life out in real time.
Hosted by two moms navigating the chaos of everyday life, this podcast is a mix of deep conversations, unfiltered thoughts, laughter, healing, growth and the kind of topics women usually only talk about in voice notes or late-night chats with friends.
No perfection. No pretending. Just real talk. 🤍

title: The Shakedown Archives
author: Daniel Heller
description: The Shakedown Archives is a forensic documentary podcast about the Grateful Dead - the band, the scene, and the fifty-year tape culture that kept it alive. Every episode reconstructs a single story from the record: a song, a night, a turning point, a person. No myth-making, no jam-band cliches - just the documented history, sourced and told straight, from the Acid Tests to the last note at Soldier Field. Companion video on Spotify and YouTube; deep-dive data at theshakedownarchives.com.

title: Kat on the Loose
description:
Welcome to Kat on the Loose — the global Top Relationships podcast that’s raw, unfiltered, and never scripted. I’m Kat Zammuto, your no-holds-barred host guiding you through the realest stories on love, sex, heartbreak, empowerment, and bouncing back. I am a woman who lost everything and rebuilt her life from the ground up - so if you’re done playing small, done settling, and ready to upgrade your standards—and your life—this is your show!!

New episodes weekly every Wednesdays and video episodes on Youtube Kat on the Loose Show

🔞 NSFW | 🎧 Available on Apple, Spotify, iHeartRadio & more

title: מג’נרטים
author: Entrée Capital
description:
איך הופכים טכנולוגיה פורצת דרך לחברת ענק, ואיך האקו-סיסטם הישראלי יכול להוביל את המהפכה?
ברוכים הבאים ל פודקאסט מג'נרטים, פלייבוק האסטרטגיה שלכם לעולם הבינה המלאכותית (AI)

בכל פרק אנחנו לוקחים חברה בינה מלאכותית מובילה מהעולם, צוללים מאחורי הקלעים שלה, ומפרקים את מהלכי המוצר, הגו טו מרקט, הכלכלה ומלחמת הטאלנטים שהפכו אותה להצלחה – או לכישלון מהדהד. 
בלי סיסמאות מפוצצות ובגובה העיניים. מביאים לכם את הלקחים, התובנות והכלים הפרקטיים שאתם חייבים להכיר כדי לבנות, להצמיח ולנווט את הסטארטאפ שלכם. 
פודקאסט ליזמים, אנשי טק, וכל מי שרוצה להבין לאן העולם האמיתי של הבינה המלאכותית (AI)

title: The Lair of Lynch.
author: Jason "The Vern" Hemming
description: The Lair of Lynch is a podcast all about exploring the movies, the televison and art of director David Lynch.  From Eraserhead to Twin Peaks: The Return, we will review all aspects of the great art he has created.

title: IRMAĞA YÜRÜYEN YAĞMUR DAMLASI
author: Serdar ÇALIŞKAN
description: "Harika bir kitap! Çocuklara katkısı çok büyük; pedagojik açıdan oldukça yararlı ve zengin bir içeriğe sahip. Hikâyesinin içtenliği sayesinde çocukların severek ve ilgiyle okuyacaklarına emin olduğum, kütüphanelerinde mutlaka bulunması gereken kıymetli bir eser."

title: Kanazawa Guide Podcast by Yuri
author: Yuri @Kanazawa Private Tour Guide
description:
"Kanazawa Guide Podcast" takes you deep into the heart of Kanazawa, Japan.🌸
Host Yuri, born and raised in Kanazawa and work as a local private tour guide, has real conversations with the people who live and work in this city — sharing their stories, passion, and culture. In Japanese and English, with a dream of becoming a small bridge between local hearts and global travelers
🗺 Yuri's Tour : https://yuri.kanazawa-guide.com/
🛠 Co-producer: Yuuta｜Engineer & creator, Kanazawa
🍵 Yuta's Tea ceremony Ceremony: getyourguide.com/kanazawa-l32537/t647215/
Instagram Account: @kanazawatrip_yuri

title: Forging Competitors
author: DK
description: Forging Competitors is the podcast where coaches, athletes, and parents learn what it really takes to build winners. We dive into the mindset, discipline, leadership, and culture that develop tougher competitors and championship programs. Hosted by Derrek Kaier.

title: PRIME SPACE
description: PRIME SPACE | Advanced Conversations in Coaching is a podcast for professional coaches who are committed to deepening their coaching mindset, coaching presence, and coaching mastery. Hosted by Elias Scultori, MCC, each episode explores the deeper layers of the coaching craft—where mindset, skill, presence, and humanity meet in real client conversations. PRIME SPACE brings together working professional coaches—from ACC to MCC—and thoughtful voices from across the coaching profession to explore what mastery in coaching truly looks like in practice. Through honest, reflective, and sometimes provocative dialogue, the show examines topics such as the true coaching mindset, the evolution of coaching skills beyond the ICF competencies, coach presence, and the ongoing development of professional coaches. If you are a trained coach, leadership coach, or coaching professional looking to stretch your thinking, refine your craft, and engage with the deeper questions shaping the future of coaching, welcome to PRIME SPACE.

title: The Radical's Memoir: Shaping a Life for Justice
description: Embark on an unforgettable emotional adventure with Emmy, a brave and lovable dog, as she journeys from a noisy animal shelter to finding her true, forever home. Through ups and downs, moves, and marriages, young listeners will travel alongside Emmy as she learns to navigate big life changes, heartbreak, and new beginnings. By the end of this season, kids will transform from passive listeners into compassionate, resilient storytellers who understand the true meaning of family and unconditional love.  Listeners will walk away with a heart-centered empathy toolkit that helps them process difficult emotions like grief, fear of change, and anxiety about new environments. Children will learn practical social-emotional skills for navigating blended family dynamics, sharing personal space, and welcoming new friends, just like Emmy does with Buddy and Chloe. Additionally, young minds will gain a foundational understanding of animal rescue, pet responsibility, and how patience and training can turn any chaotic house into a harmonious home.  This heartwarming podcast is tailor-made for curious, animal-loving children who are learning to navigate their own big life transitions, whether that means moving to a new town, adjusting to a blended family, or welcoming a new pet. It is the perfect audio sanctuary for families to listen to together, sparking meaningful conversations about love, adaptability, and belonging. If your child has ever felt a little out of place and dreamed of finding where they truly fit in, Emmy’s story is waiting to welcome them home.

title: Measured Twice, Live Once
author: Drew
description:
Measured Twice, Live Once is a podcast about rebuilding.
Raised in a family of craftsmen, I learned to work with my hands at an early age. After losing my tools and starting over, I am rebuilding my woodshop one piece at a time.
With 12 years of sobriety, living with Multiple Sclerosis, and managing ADHD, this podcast explores what was lost, what was kept, and what I am building now.

title: I Guess I’ll Say Something
author: Ari D
description: It has been years since I said that I wanted to start a podcast, but mental illness be tripping. Well, I guess I am doing it. I Guess I’ll Say Something is about me allowing myself to be open about anything and everything that my mind can think of. Think of this podcast as a sneak peek into my journal.

title: The Entrepreneurs
author: Stuart McNish
description:
A new show and online series, set to change the way we see and appreciate the women and men who drive our economy.


Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: CLIMB by VSC
author: Jay Kapoor
description: CLIMB is about how AI is changing what we build and what we back. Each week, host Jay Kapoor sits down with the operators and investors working at the very edge of technology today, for honest conversations on where the next great companies come from. A show from VSC Ventures.

title: Camp Heirloom Radio
author: Tonia
description: Camp Heirloom Radio takes you behind the booths of the Camp Heirloom Market. Join us as we chat with makers, artists, vintage curators, and small business owners about their creative journeys, lessons learned, market experiences, and the stories behind their work. Whether you're a seasoned vendor, dreaming of starting your own business, or simply love handmade and vintage treasures, you'll find inspiration, practical advice, and a sense of community here.

title: Silver Horde
description: The Silver Horde , is set in Kalvik, a fictionalized community in Bristol Bay, Alaska, and tells the story of a down on his luck gold miner who discovers a greater wealth in Alaska's run of salmon (silver horde) and decides to open a cannery. To accomplish this he must overcome the relentless opposition of the "salmon trust," a fictionalized Alaska Packers' Association, which undercuts his financing, sabotages his equipment, incites a longshoremen's riot and bribes his fishermen to quit. The story line includes a love interest as the protagonist is forced to choose between his fiance, a spoiled banker's daughter, and an earnest roadhouse operator, a woman of "questionable virtue." (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Living Room Clutter
author: Living Room Clutter
description: Welcome to Living Room Clutter, the ultimate podcast for peripheral rhythm gaming. We are diving deep into the past, present, and future of the note-highway. Whether you are nostalgic for the golden era of Guitar Hero and Rock Band, grinding customs on Clone Hero and YARG, or tracking every update for the upcoming revival title Stage Tour, this is your home for everything five-fret.

title: Dead America - Serie de audiolibros de zombis
author: Derek Slaton
description: La serie Dead America se puede encontrar completa en este canal. El virus zombi de Texas se está propagando rápidamente por toda la nación. A medida que los infectados se convierten en veloces devoradores de carne, los supervivientes de todo el país se unen para combatir la embestida de los muertos vivientes. Dead America aborda todos los aspectos del brote zombi: desde sus orígenes en Texas, hasta la respuesta militar en las Carolinas y el corazón del país, pasando por los civiles de distintas regiones que enfrentan amenazas tanto vivas como no muertas. La acción es rápida, frenética y no da tregua.

title: Fidelity
description: The small Midwestern town of Freeport was scandalized years ago when Ruth Holland, then a young girl, ran away to the West with a married man. Now that she's returned home to take care of her dying father, she faces some hard truths about who her true friends are and where her life is headed. (Summary by Arielle Lipshaw)

title: Victorian Tales Romance - Historical Romantic Stories Short Audiobooks
description: Victorian Romance Tales - Historical Romantic Stories. Short stories on Spotify in the Victorian Era, Romance from Victorian London Sleep Stories and Historical Romance From Youtube videos now also on Spotify!

title: Trail of the Hawk: a Comedy of the Seriousness of Life
description: Trail of the Hawk, narrated by Mike Vendetti aka Miketheauctioneer, is Sinclair Lewis’ second novel published under his own name. It was not at the time of release a smashing success as his later works, Babbitt, Elmer Gantry, and others, but his style and immense talent are certainly evident. We travel with The Hawk, or Carl Ericson, son of Norwegian immigrants, born at the end of the 19th century through the first three adventurous periods of the young man’s life. We see him as an adventurous boy, running away with Gertie Cowels, the girl that lives in the big house in the better part of town, only to be rescued by Bone Stillman, a backwoods philosopher and influence on Carl. We travel with him as he leaves his childhood, Gertie and Joralemon behind, for a short attempt at higher education, then to pursue the hobo life, a Bowery bartender, an engineer in Panama and an aviator. It is during the aviator phase that he becomes Hawk Ericson barnstorming his way across America, an exciting but dangerous lifestyle. Leaving aviation after too many close calls, and the loss of his best friend, he becomes an automobile man, and moves into phase three.It is in phase three, love adventure, that we find The Hawk in New York, mingling with polite society quite by accident. He is smitten by Ruth Winslow, while riding on a streetcar after a fight with Gertie, and follows Ruth to a party which he crashes.I truly enjoyed narrating this novel, and believe the listener will enjoy it, a trip into post Victorian, pre-Jazz Age America.(Summary by Mike Vendetti)

title: The Buffer State
author: Sasha Shtern
description: The Buffer State is about geopolitical power in the age of computation. We examine how software, computing power, and access to space reshape the instruments of trade, deterrence, and coercion.

title: Changed Man And Other Tales
description: Eleven short stories. - Summary by david wales

title: Life's Little Ironies
description: Eighteen short stories by a master story teller. - Summary by david wales

title: Spirit of the Town
description: A novel presentation in fiction form of the impulse and desire which mold the lives of men -- From the title page

title: 1916: First Chapters Collection
description: These are first chapters to books first published in 1916. Readers, following is a tentative list culled from Wikipedia's article on the year in Literature; most of the works are to be found on Project Gutenberg. - Summary by Matt Pierard

title: Thurley Ruxton
description: This is a rags to riches romance about an exceedingly beautiful, poor, young girl (Thurley Ruxton) who is mentored by one of New York’s elite hostesses. In order to draw them into her social circle, she allows all the famous and moneyed populous of Gothem to believe that Thurley is the princess Thurvinia hiding in New York to escape an arranged marriage. This causes all manner of high drama and romance with a suitable ending. (Summary by Paul Hansen)

title: Jennie Gerhardt
description: This is a story of an innocent, caring, beautiful young girl from and extremely poor family who throughout her life is drawn into affairs with two different men from a much higher social class. How members of her family, the family of one of the wealthy men, and society in general react to her situation is the basis of this story. (Summary by Richard Kilmer)

title: Lady Bridget in the Never-Never Land
description: Following a failed love affair in England, Lady Bridget O'Hara accepts an invitation to travel to colonial Australia as companion to Lady Rosamund Tallant, the wife of the newly-appointed governor of Leichardt's Land. In Leichardt's Town, Lady Bridget, also known as Biddy, is reunited with her old friend and collaborator, Joan Gildea, special correspondent for The Imperialist newspaper. While visiting Joan, Biddy meets Colin McKeith, a roughly-hewn, Scottish-born pioneer, drover, miner, sometime-politician, and magistrate in the north-eastern colony. Biddy and Colin fall in love: she with the adventure a life with him promises, he with an ideal of her noble heritage. In spite of Joan Gildea's misgivings, Biddy and Colin are soon married and leave Leichardt's Town to travel several days north to Colin's cattle property in a region known as the Leura. As Biddy and Colin embark on their life together, the contemporary issues of colonial Australia are revealed: the extreme environment, labour shortages and organisation, police brutality, immigration policy, and the plight of Australia's First Peoples. The couple discover fundamental differences in their perspectives on many topics. When Bridget's former love, Willoughby Maule, newly-widowed and affluent, visits her in the Leura, the couple's strained relationship is further tested. (Summary by Kirsty Leishman)

title: Eye of Dread
description: The Civil War is upon the United States, and the town of Leauvite has sent away its young men, among them Peter Junior and Richard Kildene. When they leave, Betty Ballard is but a child, but by the time the war has ended, she has become something else entirely. Wounded, Peter Junior finds his time at Betty's father's studio, learning to become an artist, until one day he proposes to her after deciding to go to France. Of course, she agrees to wait for him. Meanwhile, Richard, who has decided to work on the railways, expects that when he gets back, that Betty will be waiting for him. With these complications, problems begin to arise... (Summary by Chelsea)

title: HFY Stories Bard
author: codeworkbench
description: HFY Stories Bard - Original Short Stories from the Space Realm for all the inhabitants of Planet Earth (stories are original and not being copied from HFY Subreddit). Short Sci-Fi Stories Audiobooks story reading and storytelling for adults and for teens.

title: Financier
description: In Philadelphia, Frank Cowperwood, whose father is a banker, makes his first money by buying cheap soaps on the market and selling it back with profit to a grocer. Later, he gets a job in Henry Waterman & Company, and leaves it for Tighe & Company. He also marries an affluent widow, in spite of his young age. Over the years, he starts embezzling municipal funds. In 1871, the Great Chicago Fire redounds to a stock market crash, prompting him to be bankrupt and exposed. Although he attempts to browbeat his way out of being sentenced to jail by intimidating Mr Stener, politicians from the Republican Party use their influence to use him as a scapegoat for their own corrupt practices. Meanwhile, he has an affair with Aileen Butler, a young girl, subsequent to losing faith in his wife. She vows to wait for him after his jail sentence. Her father, Mr Butler dies; she grows apart from her family. Frank divorces his wife. Sometime after being released, he invests in stocks subsequent to the Panic of 1873, and becomes a millionaire again. He decides to move out of Philadelphia and start a new life in the West. (Introduction by Wikipedia) This is Book 1 of Trilogy of Desire.

title: Human Affairs
description: Vincent O'Sullivan was a celebrated writer of decadent and morbid fiction of his time, a notable contemporary of Oscar Wilde. While O'Sullivan is perhaps most well-known for his supernatural tales of horror, here represented by the novella "Vershoyle's House", this collection of stories shows us all facets of O'Sullivan's personality in his inimitable demi-monde style. (Summary by Ben Tucker)

title: Radio Cop
description: Now he was not only a police announcer, but a news reporter, and the biggest story of the year was breaking right under his nose. As he described the scene, there was a ring in his voice that brought his listeners up wide-eyed. - Summary extracted from the text

title: Short Science Fiction Collection 056
description: Science fiction is a genre encompassing imaginative works that take place in this world or that of the author’s creation where anything is possible. The only rules are those set forth by the author. The speculative nature of the genre inspires thought and plants seeds that have led to advances in science. The genre can spark an interest in the sciences and is cited as the impetus for the career choice of many scientists. It is a playing field to explore social perspectives, predictions of the future, and engage in adventures unbound into the richness of the human mind. - Summary by Amy Gramour

title: The Lundy Murders
author: Bryan Bruce
description:
A 3-part podcast in which Bryan Bruce follows up on the Lundy Murders episode of his INVESTIGATOR cold case television series . 
Why did two juries 13 years apart find Mark Lundy guilty of the brutal killing of his wife Christine and 7-year-old daughter Amber?
For crime scene images and police interviews visit thelundycase.substack.com

title: Short Stories from Locomotive Engineers Journal, Volume 52
description: These short stories are taken from the Locomotive Engineers Journal, Volume 52 which covers the year, 1918. Don't expect any trains -- these are short works on many subjects, including romance, adventure, humor, etc. -- of 5 to 15 minutes in length, by minor pulp fiction writers of the day, including Agnes Brogan, Pauline Richards, Warren Miller, Clarissa Mackie, and Xeno Putnam. Putnam wrote a couple of non-fiction works on farm equipment; Mackie was a prolific writer whose work appeared in several magazines and newspapers. I have created an e-book version of this set for easier viewing and placed it on Archive's Community Texts here . (Summary by Matt Pierard)

title: Short Stories From Life
description: Life Magazine held a short story contest. Stories must be no more than 1500 words. Authors were paid for the number of words under that maximum. The shortest story accepted, and the one where the author received the highest payout, came in at 76 words. The stories published here where considered the best of the over 30,000 submissions. This volume presents the 81 best stories including the prize winning stories: (First Prize) Thicker Than Water by Ralph Henry Barbour and George Randolph Osborne, (Second Prize) The Answer by Harry Stillwell Edwards, and (Third Prize - tie) Her Memory by Dwight M. Wiley and Business and Ethics by Redfield Ingalls. - Summary taken from the Preface

title: Royal Highness
description: Royal Highness is the story of Prince Klaus Heinrich, a member of a struggling German duchy and an exotic American heiress who comes to live as his neighbor. The novel is a microcosm of Europe before World War I, with Mann's depiction of a decaying society that is rejuvenated by modern forces. A true modern day fairy tale. (Summary by Margaret Espaillat)

title: Poor White: a Novel
description: This is a story of the effect of industrialization on a small town and its people, around the beginning of the 20th Century. The lead character is Hugh McVey—a shy, lazy, fairly ignorant, dreamy young man. Most of the story takes place in the little town of Bidwell, Ohio, where Hugh gets a job as a telegraph operator. He does discover he has a talent with calculations and drawings that enable him to become an inventor. In time he will become a millionaire. The story has many characters, and shows the negative effects on each of them, as the factories come to their town. The pioneering, agrarian spirit of each is slowly transformed; greed is the primary driver. Hugh sees this transformation but does not understand it. Several of the townspeople take advantage of him; his inventions of farm machinery are stolen. Only Clara Butterworth seems to bring some sanity; she marries Hugh, and they are happy for a time. As the novel approaches an end, however, there seem to be no bright spots. Labor fights, terrible graft, terrible crime, even murder come to Bidwell. The author paints a negative picture of a loss of innocence, as the nation becomes industrialized. Written in 1920, Sherwood Anderson could have seen only a small fraction of the changes to come. The magnitude, good and bad, would no doubt astound him. (Note: The novel is divided into “books”; however, there is no Book 5 between Book 4 and 6; hence I have so narrated it.) (Summary by Bob Rollins)

title: Souls for Sale
description: Perhaps the most commercially successful Hollywood novel of the 1920s, Rupert Hughes' Souls for Sale is a direct response to contemporaneous charges of the film industry's moral laxities and predilections toward vice. Remember "Mem" Steddon, the pious and steadfast daughter of a religious firebrand who preaches about the sins of Hollywood, is forced to migrate to the west coast after discovering that she's become pregnant out of wedlock. On her journey, Mem runs into the inhabitants of a movie colony and soon befriends many of these delightful (if somewhat peculiar) "film folk." She finds temporary employment as a film extra and soon develops aspirations to become a star herself. But Mem soon discovers that Hollywood is a far different place than she had originally imagined and that the road to film stardom is not straightforward or easy to navigate. This novel was the basis for the popular 1923 film adaptation, which starred an up-and-coming Eleanor Boardman and was directed by Rupert Hughes himself. (Summary by ChuckW)

title: Windy McPherson's Son
description: Windy McPherson's Son is the story of Sam McPherson's rise in the world of business and search for emotional enlightenment in later life. McPherson starts out as an ambitious newsboy in Caxton, Ohio, with drunkard of a father who constantly embarrasses him. Eventually, after his mother's death and an episode with a middle-aged schoolteacher, McPherson leaves Caxton for Chicago. In Chicago, he gets a job as a buyer of farm implements and establishes his reputation in business. While his professional life is blossoming, his personal life suffers. After meeting Sue Rainey, the daughter of his boss Colonel Rainey, they get married and twice fail to have children. Following a business deal that forces his father-in-law out of his own company, McPherson and Sue Rainey separate. One day, once McPherson had become quite wealthy, he gets a telegram saying that Colonel Rainey committed suicide. This causes Sam to realize that he is unhappy with his life. This feeling inspires him to leave Chicago and travel all over becoming involved in various adventures. Finally, McPherson's comes across a promiscuous and alcoholic mother of three children. A deal is made and McPherson gets custody of the children. Showing up with the children at Sue's current place of residence, the five of them become family. ( Wikipedia)

title: Solex Podcast
author: Solex LLC
description: The Solex Podcast showcases a variety of exciting health-related topics from the premier health technology company. Our Solex Nation community is quickly growing as our distributors (QLAs) share our life-changing products with others & grow businesses of their own. Solex is dedicated to life optimization through the science of frequency & energy. Our bestselling AO Scan technology is an incredible educational tool for the body. Learn more at solexglobal.com.

title: Mr. Weston's Good Wine
description: This 1927 novel describes an evening in 1923 when Mr. Weston, who is apparently a wine merchant, but is evidently God, visits the fictional village of Folly Down in Dorset, and meets some of its individuals, whose backgrounds and lives leading up to this day are described during the course of the novel. Mr. Weston's colleague is named Michael, which is an allusion to the Archangel. For a while time stands still, and these individuals, according to their possessing qualities of good or evil, find their ultimate reward. - Summary by Wikipedia and David Wales

title: Whirligigs
description: A collection of short stories. - Summary by Richard Kilmer

title: Blazed Trail Stories and Stories Of The Wild Life
description: Thirteen short stories by a popular writer of the early 20th century (not to be confused with an earlier book Blazed Trail). White's books were popular at a time when America was losing its vanishing wilderness. He was a keen observer of the beauties of nature and human nature, yet could render them in a plain-spoken style. Based on his own experience, whether writing camping journals or Westerns, he included pithy and fun details about cabin-building, canoeing, logging, gold-hunting, and guns and fishing and hunting. He also interviewed people who had been involved in the fur trade, the California gold rush and other pioneers which provided him with details that give his novels verisimilitude. He salted in humor and sympathy for colorful characters such as canny Indian guides and "greenhorn" campers who carried too much gear. - Summary by Wikipedia and david wales

title: Poison Island
description: Mysterious old Captain Coffin not only has a wild story about a Honduran island where treasure lies, he has a map. But they must be wary because it also holds “a poison that kills a man and keeps him fresh as paint”. Nothing can hold Harry back from tagging along and meeting up with murder, intrigue, a collection of colorful characters, and a vociferous parrot. - Summary by Amy Gramour

title: Valley of Fear (Version 4 Dramatic Reading)
description: The Valley of Fear is the fourth novel Arthur Conan Doyle wrote about private detective Sherlock Holmes. First published serially in The Strand Magazine between September 1914 and May 1915, this novel was published as a book three months before its serialization was complete. Doyle went on to write more Sherlock Holmes stories, but The Valley of Fear was his last novel about the character. This novel is the only one of the four in which criminal mastermind Professor Moriarty appears, and one of only two stories by Doyle in which Moriarty plays a direct role; despite the prominence that later Sherlock Holmes adaptations have given to Moriarty, this antagonist plays a minor role in Doyle’s original oeuvre. In writing The Valley of Fear, Doyle used a similar structure to that of his first Sherlock Holmes novel, A Study in Scarlet; both novels are divided into two seven-chapter parts, the first part focusing on Sherlock Holmes’s investigation of a murder and the second part exploring the murder’s backstory. Inspiration for The Valley of Fear came from the life of real-world private detective James McParland and his investigation of the Molly Maguires, an Irish secret society. Further inspiration came from Groombridge Place, a moated manor house in England on which the novel’s Birlstone Manor is closely based. This novel has sometimes attracted controversy due to its negative depiction of trade unions. For example, the 1930s radio series The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes dramatized all of Doyle’s canonical Sherlock Holmes short stories and novels except The Valley of Fear, which was excluded because its depiction of trade unions was considered too political. Consequently, a radio adaptation of this novel did not appear until 1960. Other media adaptations have included films, television episodes, and a stage play. - Summary by David Purdy Cast List: Sherlock Holmes: Peter Yearsley Dr. Watson: David Purdy John Douglas: Algy Pug Jack McGinty: Greg Giordano Cecil Barker: larryhayes7 Alec MacDonald: Adrian Stephens White Mason: Craig Franklin Mike Scanlan: ToddHW Ivy Douglas: Jenn Broda Ettie Shafter: TJ Burns Ted Baldwin: Yoganandh T Carter: bwg1214 Ames: James R. Hedrick Brother Morris: Chuck Williamson Tiger Cormac: rssmith121999 Harraway: Farzan Sergeant Wilson: john warren hart Teddy Marvin: Joe Bergin Arthur Willaby: chuckconvr Gower: Beeswaxcandle Manders: lorda Fred Porlock: Larry Wilson Dr. Wood: Michael Broomhill Policeman Evans: Jaime Kurzweg Policeman Hunt: Ted Perkins Jenkins: KevinS Jacob Shafter: arbycoors James Stanger: Tad Davis J.W. Windle: Tomas Peter Reilly: Scott Caulkins Mansel: Alan Dove Young Wilson: John Payton James Murdoch: Peter Dann Mrs. Larbey: Carol Sutton Josiah H. Dunn: Rich Brown Lawler: Keith Salis Operator: Michelle Purdy Telegrapher: redrun Professor Moriarty: Cavaet Narrator: Michele Eaton

title: Arrowsmith
description: This 1926 Pulitzer Prize winning novel centers on the title character, a promising medical student who, as a doctor and following several intervening ventures, becomes a medical researcher in New York. A widespread killer plague takes him to a Caribbean island to produce and inject sera and do research. Fascinating characters, some professional, others romantic, impact his life. Striking similarities of the epidemic in this novel to the pandemic of the 2020's may today seem prophetic. The author won the 1930 Nobel Prize in literature, chiefly for "Arrowsmith". (Lee Smalley)

title: Measure Of A Man; A Tale Of The Big Woods
description: A tale of a singular man at work among lumber-jacks. Duncan's droll style and tongue-in-cheek viewpoint keep the reader and listener much entertained. - Summary by Book preface and david wales

title: Prodigal Daughters
description: A frank look at the revolt of the younger generation following World War I, the book follows the Trelawney family. The father looks eagerly forward to his return home after serving an important but harrowing stint in the army. What he finds at home is not what he expects, as his two daughters test the boundaries of new morals, ethics, and dress. Many of the generational and class issues central to the theme continue to resonate in families and society. The book was made into a silent movie starring Gloria Swanson in 1923. Summary by Kate Follis

title: Initiation
description: A tale of all types of human pain (physical, spiritual, and psychological), this book teaches what it means to be truly human. An interesting and witty story although the plot is somewhat grim. - Summary by Mozartjr

title: Emily Climbs, Version 3
description: Teenage orphan Emily Starr of New Moon has always wanted to be a great writer. But her old-fashioned aunt, who has never liked Emily’s writing, refuses to let Emily go to high school unless she stops! Shrewsbury brings new friends, new adventures, and new enemies, and the town is scandalized by some of Emily’s exploits. Perhaps the hardest trial is having to board with her Aunt Ruth. Or is it her promise to Aunt Elizabeth? But Emily’s troubles are only the beginning of her climb to success … and perhaps romance. - Summary by Rachel This recording was originally published on Legamus.eu and was later released on LibriVox when US copyright permitted.

title: Some Everyday Folk and Dawn
description: It is 1904 and women's suffrage has hit the small town of Noonoon. Though the election campaigners preen themselves for the women's vote, the fight isn't entirely won, for the male residents are bristling at this threat to their supremacy. And down at Clay's there are other problems too: Dawn is now a young woman and in these days of slender chances Grandma Clay must keep an eye on the marriage market. But Dawn, lively and outspoken wants a career on the stage. - Summary by Trove - National Library of Australia

title: Little Stories
description: Physician and author S. Weir Mitchell brings us a short collection of stories of the human condition. Through diverse settings as the mystical Arabian desert to a lonely park bench, from a jocular sea port to a dusty library packed with archaic tomes written in foreign tongues, S. Weir Mitchell shows us what it means to live as others live. Through hauntings both literal and metaphorical, through desperate acts and moral dilemmas, we are shown through these slight sketches that life is as complicated or simple as we choose to make it. (Summary by Ben Tucker)

title: Triumph of the Egg: A Book of Impressions from American Life In Tales and Poems
description: “The Triumph of the Egg” is a collection of stories and poems by Sherwood Anderson. Abandoning the interconnected quality of his more famous “Winesburg, Ohio,” the author adopts a variety of perspectives and settings while exploring similar themes: personal growth, disillusionment, loneliness, and urban-rural contrast. In the North American Review, critic Lawrence Gilman wrote, “Mr. Anderson has achieved a beauty that irradiates his page.” Though largely overshadowed by that celebrated, earlier book, “The Triumph of the Egg” remains a foundational work for Modernist literature, proven by its winning the first annual Dial Award from the influential journal The Dial. NOTE TO LISTENERS: The second story in this collection, “I Want to Know Why,” contains language that may be offensive. (summary by Ben Adams)

title: Little Brown Jug at Kildare
description: Two men in search of adventure and romance set off on their own journeys. Only to find themselves been drawn together to solve issues arising in the States of North and South Carolina when both of their Governors go missing.

title: Dark Star
description: Ruhannah “Rue” Carew, who dreams of becoming an artist, has grown up listening to her father tell the story of how he came by a wooden box and its unusual contents, which include a bronze figure of a god and the diary of a departed friend of her father’s. One day Rue meets an unscrupulous man named Eddie Brandes, who is so taken with her that he hides his true nature in order to win the trust and regard of her and her parents, with an eye to marrying her. But neither Rue nor her parents suspect that Eddie is already married to an actress named Ilse Dumont. Rue learns the truth about Eddie under devastating circumstances, leaving her at a loss as to what to do with her life in the aftermath of her discovery. But with the help of her friend Jim Neeland, Rue finds herself crossing the Atlantic headed to Paris in order to pursue her dream and study art. Several years pass. It is the eve of the Great War. One day, Jim receives an urgent message that he is to find the box in Rue’s old house and bring it with him to her in Paris.
